headline: Econet’s ZSE market value hits $4bn
content: Econet Wireless Zimbabwe has become the largest company on the Zimbabwe Stock Exchange by market value at $4 billion, accounting for 19 percent of ZSE's total market capitalisation and replacing Delta Corporation Limited which held the top position for years, with the telecoms giant see-sawing between pole position and fourth position over the past three years—slipping to fourth in January 2016, rising again, and being replaced by its fintech business Cassava Smartech on December 18, 2018 before reclaiming top position. Delta Corporation is now valued at $3.8 billion with Cassava Smartech at $3.86 billion, completing the top three counters, while Innscor maintains fourth position at $985 million and BAT completes the top five with $680 million as the market's most expensive stock at $33. Delta had reclaimed its long-held top position for only a fortnight before the shift in positions following a rally in both Econet and Cassava, with the beverages maker's cash generative capacity helping it survive the turbulent economic environment despite foreign currency shortages affecting local industry and capacity utilisation dropping to 42 percent in November 2018 from 48 percent in August 2018. Delta reported revenue growth of 5 percent for Q3 2018 and 24 percent for nine months on growth in beer businesses (lager +27%, sorghum +15% with Chibuku Super contributing 85% of volume) weighed down by depressed soft drinks outturn. Econet's earnings are anticipated to suffer following an internet blackout during violent protests in January 2018 . No CBZ Holdings or African Distillers Limited is mentioned in this article.

headline: Delta reverses forex decision
content: Delta Corporation reversed its January 2, 2019 decision to sell all products exclusively in foreign currency effective January 4, 2019 following crisis talks on the evening of January 3, 2019 attended by Vice President Constantino Chiwenga, Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe Governor Dr John Mangudya, Finance and Economic Development Minister Professor Mthuli Ncube, and Delta chief executive Mr Pearson Gowero, with the parties issuing a joint statement that Delta withdraws the notice to sell its products exclusively in hard currency in the spirit of the multi-currency framework and the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe will endeavour to provide the foreign currency required to ensure that Delta continues to trade on the current basis. Delta had argued it required US$2 million per month to buy raw materials including concentrates and granules for plastic bottles, that its business had been adversely affected by foreign currency shortages resulting in failure to meet orders and soft drinks being out of stock for prolonged periods, that the fiscal and monetary policy frameworks announced in October 2018 did not provide for easy access to foreign currency by non-exporters, and that the company had invested over US$600 million in plant, equipment, vehicles and ancillary services since 2009 which needed protection. The company had stopped producing soft drinks in November 2018 and only released limited stock on December 24, 2018 for Christmas, with the proposed prices set at 50c for 300ml bottle, 60c for 330ml can, US$1 for 500ml PET, 80c for 375ml returnable lager, US$1.50 for 750ml returnable, and US$1 for 340ml returnable. Minister Ncube appealed to the private sector to be patient while Government worked on fiscal consolidation and requisite monetary reforms, stating 'They should not rush ahead to choose the currency they think the whole country should adopt.' This resolution followed The Herald's highly critical editorial commentary earlier that day (DLTA014) which accused Delta of 'cheating' customers, farmers, suppliers and workers and suggested consumers pay Delta in kind. Delta's 2018 Review of Operations confirmed foreign supplier arrears totalled US$41 million .

headline: Dairibord Zimbabwe shuts down 50% of its plants
content: Dairibord Zimbabwe Holdings has shut down four of its seven national plants, operating at just 13 percent production capacity, following a catastrophic decline in the national dairy herd from 192,000 to 32,000 cattle and a corresponding drop in annual milk production from 150 million litres to 68 million litres. Chief executive officer Anthony Mandiwanzira announced the closures, attributing the crisis to under-funding from Government, the impacts of drought and disease, and the failure of authorities to respond to a 2018 request for foreign currency to import heifers that could produce milk. The company is now retrenching workers. This crisis was extensively documented throughout 2019: Mandiwanzira told parliamentarians that the national milk deficit of 50 million litres was being filled by powdered milk imports costing over US$7 million per month , and that Dairibord had mothballed plants in Bulawayo, Gweru, and Kadoma (which used to make the best cheese in southern Africa), with Chipinge and Mutare plants operating below potential . Treasury confirmed in its First Quarter Bulletin that the national dairy herd stood at 38,201 in early 2019 and that over 5,000 cattle were lost to drought-related deaths between January and April . The company was forced to adopt a 'hold and survival' strategy for the remainder of 2019, focusing on supporting milk supply chain viability, cost reduction, and reducing foreign currency liabilities , while contending with nationwide power outages forcing reliance on costly diesel generators . Despite the devastation, Dairibord's export strategy showed resilience, with foreign currency earnings surging 100 percent to US$3.4 million for FY2019

headline: JUST IN: Masiyiwas avail $60 million towards cholera response
content: The Higherlife Foundation, family foundation of Econet founders Strive and Tsitsi Masiyiwa, has committed an additional US$60 million of their own funds towards Zimbabwe's cholera response to be spent over five years, following an initial US$10 million investment by Econet Wireless Zimbabwe at the onset of the September 2018 cholera outbreak, of which nearly half has already been used to rehabilitate critical but dilapidated sewer infrastructure and procure emergency supplies. An emergency project is already underway to build a large public health emergency operations centre—a 'situation room' from which the Ministry of Health and Child Care can better monitor and respond to a range of disease outbreaks and public health security threats. The new funds will support a long-term roadmap to eliminate water-borne diseases, focusing on catalyzing innovative solutions to water and sanitation infrastructure problems through public private partnerships and creative financial models for sustainable municipal service provision. The commitment supports a wider cholera initiative, 'End Cholera Now: The 10 year promise', spearheaded by a multi-sector National Taskforce on Cholera Elimination with support from Higherlife Foundation, working to establish a reform agenda and convene game-changing strategic PPPs with the ultimate goal of eliminating cholera in Zimbabwe by 2030, guided by the WHO's Global Cholera Roadmap and Zimbabwe's Vision 2030. Higherlife Foundation, established over 20 years ago as an education trust, has since pivoted towards investing in human capital to develop thriving individuals and communities through impact investments in education, health, entrepreneurship, rural transformation and women's empowerment, with operations in Lesotho, Burundi and Kenya. While the Masiyiwas are founders of Econet Wireless Zimbabwe, this philanthropic commitment is made through their family foundation and does not represent a material business transaction for Econet Wireless Zimbabwe, the listed entity. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: Delta unveils scholarship scheme
content: Delta Corporation has unveiled a two-year bursary programme for a bright but underprivileged pupil who passed their 2018 Ordinary Level examinations and qualifies for Advanced Level studies in Masvingo Province, according to corporate affairs executive Ms Patricia Murambinda in a letter addressed to the provincial education director. The bursary will cover tuition fees, part of uniform costs, an allowance for textbooks and pocket money for a pupil enrolled at either a Government or mission school within the province, with Delta specifically excluding students enrolled at private schools or outside Masvingo. The company has invited the Primary and Secondary Education Ministry's Masvingo provincial office to nominate at least three pupils who scored exceptionally high passes in the November-December 2018 Ordinary Level examinations and come from a proven less privileged background, from which one student will be selected, with shortlisting not automatically guaranteeing an award and successful candidates to be contacted in writing. The closing date for nominations is February 14, 2019. This scholarship initiative adds to Delta's extensive corporate social responsibility portfolio following the December 2018 confirmation of a new three-year Premier Soccer League sponsorship deal (DLTA013), the August 2023 US$12,000 Melfort Old People's Home agricultural project donation (DLTA012), and the company's 125-year operating history in Zimbabwe with over US$600 million post-2009 investment in plant and equipment

headline: UPDATED: Battlefields declared State of Disaster
content: CBZ Treasury account designated for donations","President Mnangagwa has declared a State of Disaster under Section 27 (1) of the Civil Protection Act following the February 12, 2019 flooding of mining shafts at Cricket No. 3 and Jongwe Mining Cooperative in Mhondoro-Ngezi where an estimated 60 to 70 artisanal miners are believed to have perished, with Local Government, Public Works and National Housing Minister July Moyo announcing that Government requires at least $200,000 to pump out water, feed bereaved families and response teams, transport victims, and conduct burials in respective districts. Minister Moyo explicitly directed that cash donations and transfers should be made to the Ministry of Finance and Economic Development on Ecocash Biller Code 140286 or CBZ Treasury 066 Account Number 21537300017, Selous Branch, with Swift Code COBZZWHA and Branch Sort Code 6100, while donations in kind should be channelled through the Department of Civil Protection on the 7th Floor of Makombe Building in Harare or the Mhondoro-Ngezi District Administrator's Office in Kadoma for accountability and transparency. This follows the identical designation of CBZ Treasury Account Number 066215300017 in the February 17, 2019 Herald report (CBZ062) , confirming CBZ as the Government's preferred banking partner for national disaster relief fund collection. Mines and Mining Development Deputy Minister Polite Kambamura stated the miners were operating outside the Mines and Minerals Act along a water channel 300 metres from the shafts, with Government planning safe mining campaigns and reaction teams in strategic points, while Mashonaland West Provincial Affairs Minister Mary Mliswa-Chikoka described the tragedy as a wake-up call on health and safety standards for artisanal miners. Corporates assisting with human resources and equipment included Zimplats, Golden Valley, RioZim and Mrasta, with chief mining engineer Eng Munodawafa reporting successful de-watering of eight metres in less than two hours with hopes of retrieving bodies by February 16, 2019.

headline: PRESIDENT TOUCHED BY BATTLEFIELDS TRAGEDY
content: CBZ Treasury account designated for disaster donations","President Mnangagwa has mourned artisanal miners who perished after mining shafts at Cricket No. 3 and Jongwe Mining Cooperative in Mhondoro-Ngezi were flooded on Tuesday, February 12, 2019, with an estimated 60 to 70 people dead, eight rescued and 24 bodies retrieved, and the President declared the incident a State of Disaster in terms of Section 27 (1) of the Civil Protection Act, Chapter 10.06, instructing Mines and Mining Development Minister Winston Chitando and Local Government, Public Works and National Housing Minister July Moyo to be in-situ to monitor and supervise the operation. In a statement, Minister Moyo announced that donations in kind should be channelled through the Department of Civil Protection on the 7th Floor of Makombe Building in Harare or Mhondoro-Ngezi District Administrator's Office in Kadoma for accountability and transparency, while cash and transfers should be directed to the Ministry of Finance and Economic Development on EcoCash Biller Code 140286 or CBZ Treasury Account Number 066215300017, Selous Branch, or on Swift Code COBZZWHA and Branch Short Code 6100. CBZ Bank is named as the designated financial institution for receiving public donations to the national disaster relief effort, with the bank's account details officially published in the Government Gazette for purposes of facilitating transparent and accountable collection of funds for the victims.

headline: Dairibord doubles profits in 2018FY
content: Dairibord Holdings reported after tax profit of $6.48 million for the full year ended December 31, 2018, more than double the $1.94 million recorded in the prior year, driven by 28 percent revenue growth to $126.44 million. Chairperson Josphat Sachikonye stated the operating environment deteriorated during the period under review, particularly the second half which was characterised by worsening foreign currency shortages and rising inflation, with year-on-year inflation closing at 42.09 percent and foods and non-alcoholic beverages inflation surging to 53.68 percent, negatively impacting product supply and the cost of doing business. Total borrowings declined to $3.7 million from $7.78 million in the previous year, finance costs reduced to $570,000 from $718,000, and net cash generated from operations increased 48 percent to $9.23 million due to improved operating performance. Raw milk intake improved 20 percent compared to national growth of 14 percent, reflecting benefits of the group's milk supply development strategy. Liquid milk volumes grew 9 percent and volumes sold from continuing operations increased 3 percent, but growth was constrained by worsening foreign currency availability to buy raw and packaging materials. Sachikonye expressed expectation that the operating environment would improve in 2019 following the Monetary Policy Statement and Government's commitment to implement the Transitional Stabilisation Plan, with inflation expected to decline and aggregate demand expected to slow in the medium term. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, Econet Wireless, Seed Co, Hippo Valley Estates, BAT Zimbabwe, CAFCA Limited, OK Zimbabwe, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: SEED Co donates 60 tonnes of maize meal to Cyclone Idai survivors
content: SEED Co Zimbabwe donated 60 tonnes of maize meal to victims of Cyclone Idai in Manicaland and Masvingo provinces, with 30 tonnes allocated to Chimanimani and 30 tonnes to other affected areas, while subsidiary Quton contributed cooking oil, blankets, matemba and sugar beans. SEED Co managing director for Southern Africa Denias Zaranyika stated the disaster affecting Zimbabwe, Mozambique and Malawi was the worst in 50 years, requiring all stakeholders to come together and assist victims, with SEED Co pledging further support to Malawi and Mozambique. The article concludes by noting that 'several other companies have extended a helping hand to the victims, with giant beverages manufacturer Delta Corporation, also pledging RTGS$1.5 million towards the ongoing efforts to deal with the emergency. Delta has mobilised immediate food hand-outs, beverages for service personnel, and blankets worth RTGS$50,000.' This single-sentence mention of Delta Corporation is incidental and does not constitute material business news for Delta. The primary subject, headline and substance of the article concern SEED Co's corporate social responsibility initiative. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Econet Wireless, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: JUST IN: Econet denies cyclone relief pullout
content: Econet Wireless Zimbabwe issued a statement denying reports that it had withdrawn relief operations to Cyclone Idai victims due to alleged political interference, confirming that its teams carried out the highest number of missions in a single day on March 26, 2019 and that base camps are operating normally with a commitment to remain in the affected areas for at least 12 months. The company detailed a multi-phase relief programme: Search and Rescue (initial phase using helicopters due to impassable roads); Recovery (assisting authorities and families to identify bodies, to be scaled down in coming days); Emergency Relief (food supply to remote areas using hired helicopter fleet, to be handed over to other groups as planned); Road Access (payment for earth-moving equipment successfully opening access to most remote areas, enabling helicopter withdrawal due to cost); and Emergency Medical Support (specialist helicopters, a plane and at least 20 ambulances for evacuations of the most seriously injured to Mutare and other towns). The second phase will focus on a 12-month community recovery programme being developed with several international donors, to be announced upon completion of the recovery phase. Econet stated: 'We remain focused on our mission and are determined more than ever before to ensure that the lives of the affected people and communities return to normal as soon as is practically possible.' No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned, though Delta Corporation separately pledged RTGS$1.5 million and SEED Co donated 60 tonnes of maize meal (DLTA026, SEED001).

headline: Castle 5s launched
content: Delta Beverages launched the second edition of the Castle Lager Africa 5s, an amateur five-aside football competition, in Harare on March 13, 2019, with this year's edition incorporating women for the first time and expanding participating countries from five to seven with Nigeria, Ghana and Uganda joining. The local tournament is set to begin in three weeks' time, with Zimbabwe's top two teams joining six other country representatives in the grand finals to be held in Dar-es-Salaam, Tanzania. Delta marketing executive Irimayi Muzorewa stated that following the success of the inaugural Castle Lager Africa 5s in 2018 where Zimbabwe finished third in Lusaka, Zambia, this will be the largest five-a-side amateur football tournament in the country played across Zimbabwe, describing the format as progressive, fast-paced, fun and accessible—one of the most popular formats of the game in Africa. Muzorewa added: 'What we love about five-a-side football is how it brings people together in the spirit of friendship and camaraderie.' Former Warriors star Tinashe Nengomasha was appointed as football ambassador, mentor and spokesman for the league. This sponsorship follows Delta's extensive sports investment portfolio including the Castle Lager Premier Soccer League three-year deal (DLTA013), Soccer Stars of the Year awards (DLTA019), Chibuku Neshamwari Traditional Dance Festival (DLTA020), Chibuku Road to Fame music competitions (DLTA024), and Carling Black Label National Pool Championships (DLTA023).

headline: Econet Willowvale commissions solar plant
content: Econet Wireless Zimbabwe commissioned a multi-million dollar 466 kilowatt solar power plant at its Willowvale industrial complex, the largest commercial and industrial car-port and roof mount installation deployed in Zimbabwe, engineered and designed by Distributed Power Africa. The 466KW grid-tied solar system operates under a 25-year Independent Power Producer licence (one of the few issued by the energy and power regulator for self-consumption), generating power for self-consumption with peak capacity catering for nearly 45 percent of the business's electricity needs. Econet group CEO Douglas Mboweni stated: 'Our organisation launched a Green campaign in 2018, which has seen the company investing in alternative renewable energies, starting with six of our own business premises. The commissioning of our Willowvale solar power plant demonstrates our focus to ensure that all our offices nationwide transition to clean and reliable energy as rapidly as possible.' The plant features 1,435 panels installed at car-ports and rooftops with real-time power monitoring system, generating an estimated 780MWH of energy per annum and reducing carbon footprint by 285,000kg annually. DPA Zimbabwe CEO Divyajeet Mahajan noted the adoption of solar energy has increased significantly with new technology, expertise and viable financing solutions, positioning Zimbabwe to achieve a 33 percent reduction in emissions by 2030. DPA has installed close to 20 solar systems regionally including a 216,200 KWh per annum plant for the largest data centre facility in East Africa in Nairobi, Kenya. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: OK launches 2019 Grand Challenge promotion
content: OK Zimbabwe launched the 2019 edition of the OK Grand Challenge Jackpot Promotion, the biggest promotion in the country, on April 9, 2019 with over 150 products from 41 suppliers. Group chief executive officer Alex Siyavora stated that this year's edition was more inclusive and responsive to the needs of its market and suppliers, with changes to the prize mix following feedback to include more cash prizes while maintaining a car as the grand prize. Siyavora said: 'The market spoke, we listened and we responded. Our customers, your customers, have called for inclusion of more people in the prizes. We present to you a prize offering that has evolved from what we have done in previous years. We have therefore thrown in cash into the array of prizes to be won while maintaining a car as the grand prize.' The promotion maintains the coupon system used for the past 30 years as the mechanism for consumer participation, with affordable entry thresholds to ensure as many customers as possible can participate. The promotion runs for seven and a half weeks. Market watchers have guided consumer-oriented stocks such as OK Zimbabwe to buck the economic trend on the back of cash-generative businesses, with promotions and brand loyalty expected to play key roles in earnings. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, Econet Wireless, Seed Co, Hippo Valley Estates, BAT Zimbabwe, Dairiboard Zimbabwe, CAFCA Limited, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: Cyclone Idai: Lessons about self-reliance
content: CBZ named among financiers of United Refineries' SOBAO programme","In the aftermath of Cyclone Idai which killed hundreds and displaced thousands in Zimbabwe, Malawi and Mozambique in mid-March 2019, the article highlights corporate Zimbabwe's demonstration of self-reliance and partnership with Government in disaster response and economic development, citing United Refineries chief executive officer Busisa Moyo's philosophy that industry should not wait for Government or the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe for foreign currency to import raw materials but rather secure local inputs through strategic partnerships. CBZ Bank is explicitly named alongside Agribank and FBC Bank as one of the financial institutions partnering United Refineries in the Soya Bean Alliance Outgrowers' (SOBAO) programme, which had registered 3,300 hectares as of the 2018/19 season against a target of 7,500 hectares, working with stockfeed manufacturer National Foods to boost local soya bean production for cooking oil and livestock feed manufacturing. This initiative parallels CBZ's concurrent Command Agriculture financing mandate (CBZ030, CBZ033) and the bank's strategic focus on import substitution and agricultural lending documented throughout your corpus, including the US$50 million agri-bond (CBZ003), CBZ Agro-Yield launch (CBZ035), and the independently verified 160,000 hectares signed for maize and soya production (CBZ025). The article also documents broader corporate disaster response including Border Timbers providing coffins from available timber, Lafarge Cement's Rebuild Chimanimani and Chipinge project, and the Reboot Fund initiative by Econet, Old Mutual, United Refineries, Cassava Smartech, Liquid Telecom, Miracle Missions and Vinal Investments mobilising RTGS$100 million to rebuild over 2,000 houses, 144 businesses and 48 schools for 250,000 affected people.

headline: EcoCash fraudster steals $20k goods
content: A Harare man, Samson Chome, appeared before Harare magistrate Mr Francis Mapfumo on April 24, 2019 charged with defrauding six shops of approximately $20,000 using edited EcoCash messages between July 16-20, 2018. The State alleges Chome obtained merchant codes from cashiers, immediately edited old EcoCash messages in his phone to suit transaction values, and sent them to merchant codes, with receipts produced from POS machines. Chome then loaded the goods into a hired truck and drove away. Specific counts include Gain Cash and Cary Supermarket Graniteside ($4,995.90 for Hullet brown sugar), Windmill (Private) Limited ($8,250 for Ammonium Nitrate fertilisers), and Tredger Traders (Private) Limited ($3,516.43 for plastic chairs, four-plate stove, doors, frames, galvanised metal sheets, barbed wire and hosepipe). The offences came to light when accounts departments conducted sales reconciliations and confirmed with Econet that the transactions were never processed. The truck driver, Pio Bote, who ferried the goods, made indications at the affected businesses. Chome was remanded in custody to April 29, 2019. This case is part of a wider pattern of EcoCash-related fraud documented in 2019, including a Guruve man sentenced to 6 years for defrauding a retailer of $12,000 using similar methods (Takemore Jamu), a Bulawayo man (Bruce Chafa) accused of defrauding Reekays Supermarket of RTGS$29,100, and two Entumbane men (Israel Zondo and Muziwakhe Tshuma) arrested for faking EcoCash payments for $125 worth of drinks. The incidents prompted warnings from the Zimbabwe Republic Police for businesses to verify transactions before releasing goods.

headline: Delta cuts prices of soft drinks
content: Delta Corporation reduced recommended retail prices of soft drinks by up to 31 percent effective May 9, 2019, lowering a 300ml returnable from nearly RTGS$1.80 to RTGS$1.25 and a 2 litre PET from RTGS$6.40 to stimulate demand that had markedly declined as consumers could not afford high prices, with company secretary Alex Makamure confirming that Delta had stock in warehouses for three weeks that retailers and wholesalers were not buying due to lack of demand. The price reduction follows Delta's April 25, 2019 trading update revealing the sparkling beverages business was 'virtually closed during the quarter due to non-availability of imported raw materials', with volumes declining 89 percent compared to prior year for the fourth quarter and 44 percent for the full year ended March 31, 2019, though operations had since resumed at a slow pace with ongoing collaborative interventions together with The Coca-Cola Company to restore the business to a sustainable footing. The decision reflects waning consumer demand amid Zimbabwe's severe economic crisis, which saw Delta's full-year results published May 16, 2019 confirm sparkling beverages volumes declined 44 percent while the group's foreign currency exposure of US$85 million remained a concern due to instability of the local currency and ongoing constraints in accessing foreign currency to meet external obligations . Group revenue increased 33 percent for the quarter and 26 percent for the full year in local currency terms, while the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe indicated inflation would decline towards year-end as most companies slash prices to stimulate demand following consumer boycotts of unjustified price increases.

headline: Mlambo leaves BAT
content: British American Tobacco Zimbabwe managing director Clara Mlambo will resign effective June 30, 2019, according to a notice from acting company secretary Stephen Nyabadza, with the board expressing gratitude for her valuable contribution during her tenure and confirming that the recruitment process for a replacement is underway. Mlambo was appointed managing director on February 1, 2016, prior to which she served as managing director of British American Tobacco (Zambia) Plc listed on the Lusaka Stock Exchange. She joined the company as a management trainee and held positions including head of brands, head of trade, marketing manager before being posted to BAT South Africa as cluster manager grocery. In 2009 she became area head of brands Sub Saharan Africa and later area head of brands East and Central Africa Area based in Kenya, participating in the project team that ensured successful transition of West Africa Markets to the newly formed West Africa Area. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, Econet Wireless, Seed Co, Hippo Valley Estates, Dairiboard Zimbabwe, CAFCA Limited, OK Zimbabwe, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: Econet launches all-new YoMix app with exciting new features, improved functionality
content: Econet Wireless Zimbabwe launched an all-new YoMix app with a fresh, sleek interface and revamped menu featuring several new functions, retaining hallmark functionalities of creating personalised bundles of voice, data, SMS & Value Added Services, offering discounted YoMix bundle combinations, and the option to buy bundles for friends and loved ones. Chief Operating Officer Mr Fayaz King stated: 'We listened, we took note (of the customer feedback), and we went to work creating and developing an all-new and vastly improved app that we believe our customers deserve. We are pleased to announce that YoMix is back, it's especially suited to every individual's product tastes and preferences, and it is now a lot more user-friendly than ever before.' Chief Commercial Officer Mr Navdeep Kapur announced the new YoMix is now available for all prepaid customers (no longer limited to Elevate youth segment aged 16-34), with new features including ability to buy YoMix bundles via EcoCash, access airtime credit, recharge phones using recharge cards, airtime transfer, enhanced security with stronger customisable password feature, ability to retrieve PINs for over-scratched or damaged recharge cards, and perceptive aptitude to help customers by offering default or preset bundles mirroring customer usage behaviour. The new app is linked to the Econet Web self-care platform giving instant access to mobile number account, transaction and usage history, and customer services platforms. The app is zero-rated (no data charges after download) and available on Google Play Store for Android devices via bit.ly/2YAaIA1 or *142#, with Apple IOS App Store availability forthcoming. Mr King concluded: 'We will not stop innovating and reimagining. For us, this is about liberating and empowering the customer to basically 'cook' and 'serve' by themselves from the elaborate 'buffet' of products we offer. Gone and truly over are the days operators would impose products on customers. With YoMix, we are giving the power of choice and control back to the customer.' No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned

headline: Ok Grand Challenge ready to rumble
content: OK Zimbabwe's annual OK Grand Challenge at Borrowdale Racecourse on June 1, 2019 features a star-studded line-up of musicians including Winky D, Gemma, Killer T, Seh Calaz, Jah Signal and Judgement Yard. Winky D's manager Jonathan Banda stated that big organisations such as OK have embraced the artiste because of his high level of professionalism, noting: 'One secret of life is that; be good every time and fortune will follow you. As a band, we are guided by principles of professionalism. Winky D has been consistent in the provision of good acts... We always deliver polished acts whenever we perform and that is the secret behind our success story. We promise nothing but a top-drawer act performance on Saturday.' OK Zimbabwe announced bus transportation from 23 branches including OK Ardmbare, OK Avonlea, OK Budiriro, OK Chitungwiza, OK First Street, OK Glen Norah, OK Glen View, OK Houghton Park, OK Kuwadzana Express, OK Kuwadzana Main, OK Mabelreign, OK Mabvuku, OK Machipisa, OK Makoni, OK Malvern, OK Marimba, OK Mbare, OK Mufakose, OK Queensdale, OK St Mary's and OK Waterfalls at 08:30am on Saturday, with return buses after the main draw at 18:00 for an additional $1 fee. Admission prices: $2 (Silver ring), $3 (Gold ring), $5 (Diamond Ring), kids $1. The annual event provides significant brand exposure and customer engagement for OK Zimbabwe.

headline: Econet most popular telecom service provider: Survey
content: The Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe's 2018 Consumer Satisfaction Survey conducted by Topline Research Solutions revealed that Econet Wireless Zimbabwe is the most popular telecom service provider, used by 89 percent of household consumers and 88 percent of corporate users, with 83 percent of household consumers and 79 percent of corporate consumers reporting they 'often use' Econet's services. NetOne is used by 26 percent of household consumers and 36 percent of corporate users, with 16 percent of corporate consumers often using NetOne. Telecel is used by 10 percent of household consumers and 13 percent of corporate users, with 5 percent using Telecel frequently. An Econet spokesman stated the survey is testimony to the massive investment the company has made in its mission to provide critical services to all consumers in Zimbabwe, citing network infrastructure investment and ensuring the company keeps abreast with latest technologies for customer convenience, with the goal to continue improving customer experience. The survey overall indicated that consumers of telecommunications and postal services in Zimbabwe are generally happy with service provision. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: New dept boon for farmers
content: Secretary for Lands, Agriculture, Water, Climate and Rural Resettlement Engineer Ringson Chitsiko addressed farmers at the Seed Co Stapleford Vegetable trial station mega field day, announcing Government's intention to establish a horticulture department to cushion the sector through policies and strategies aimed at transforming agriculture from subsistence to commercial farming under Vision 2030, identifying horticulture as one of the key sectors to deliver 10 percent annual economic growth. Chitsiko hailed Seed Co as the market leader in the production and supply of a wide range of improved vegetable varieties in Zimbabwe, stating: 'I applaud Seed Co for the tremendous work they are doing in the agriculture industry; to ensure food and nutritional security through seed security and more importantly their contribution to our broader agro-based economy. We are all gathered here to share the same sentiment that this good looking piece of work is being exhibited from a Pan African seed company with Zimbabwean roots, that is working extremely well in its efforts to continue to offer a comprehensive assortment of products that meet the current market demands, both local and export. I would, therefore, always encourage the company to continue with the collaborative research efforts with their strategy partners notably HM Clause so as to leverage on their global exposure and take advantage of the facilities and skills they possess.' Seed Co's agronomy and extension services manager, Dr John Basera (later appointed Permanent Secretary), stated that vegetable farming plays a critical role in spreading cash flows on the farm, providing lucrative quick returns on investment and enabling farmers to sell daily or weekly for smooth income streams, emphasising the need for good agronomic practices and crop diversity. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, Econet Wireless, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned, though Dr Basera's subsequent career trajectory as CBZ Agro-Yield Managing Director and Permanent Secretary is documented in your corpus (CBZ061, CBZ077).

headline: EDITORIAL COMMENT : Delta shows interbank can tame pricing madness
content: Delta shows interbank can tame pricing madness; Delta switches to RTGS pricing","Delta Corporation announced on June 4, 2019 that it would now charge for all its products in local currency (RTGS dollars) with immediate effect, following significant improvement in accessibility of foreign currency on the interbank market introduced by the Reserve Bank in March 2019—reversing its January 2019 decision to sell exclusively in foreign currency which Government declared 'unacceptable and illegal' (DLTA009). The company had previously required customers to make part-payment or deposits in foreign currency with the balance in RTGS dollars to mobilise requisite foreign currency for importing key production raw materials, a practice that retailers used as justification to charge outrageous prices for virtually all Delta manufactured products. Delta had stopped production of sparkling beverages for three months to March 2019 due to unavailability of foreign currency, negatively impacting volumes, with the editorial commentary stating that the decision lends credence to Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube's argument that an efficient interbank market can resolve pricing instability, as inflationary pressures were a result of pass-through effects of currency premiums from parallel market procurement. Government had secured a US$500 million external line of credit to support efficient functioning of the interbank market, with Zimbabwe exporting US$4.1 billion in 2018 (up from US$3.5 billion in 2017) ranking 21st in Africa, yet facing forex disequilibrium causing mismatches between demand and supply that starved key sub-sectors of critical imports including fuel, medicines, raw materials and electricity. The editorial urged all economic players to emulate Delta's decision to price in RTGS dollars and use the interbank market to procure foreign currency, bringing predictability and stability in pricing and increasing confidence in and stability of the currency.

headline: President meets top US official
content: Mnangagwa raises CBZ's US$350m fine with Tibor Nagy","President Mnangagwa held a historic meeting with United States Assistant Secretary of State for African Affairs Mr Tibor Nagy on the sidelines of the 12th US-Africa Business Summit in Maputo, Mozambique—Zimbabwe's first invitation to the summit following years of diplomatic isolation under the previous regime—in a development described by Finance and Economic Development Minister Professor Mthuli Ncube as a 'milestone' in restoring normal relations with Washington. Professor Ncube confirmed that President Mnangagwa explicitly raised the issue of CBZ Bank's punitive US$350 million fine imposed by the US Office of Foreign Assets Control for ostensibly violating sanctions laws, stating: 'We also raised the issue of CBZ Bank; as you know CBZ was fined US$350 million, it is our largest bank and perhaps we need some assistance if they have to pay that amount of money; it is a lot of money. I think there was sympathy from the other side of the table.' The President updated Mr Nagy on legislative and economic reforms under the Transitional Stabilisation Programme, conveyed that 'Zimbabwe is open for business' to American companies including General Electric's involvement in infrastructure projects, and addressed the 'elephant in the room'—ZIDERA sanctions—explaining progress made on issues troubling the United States. This represents the first documented instance of CBZ's US$385 million OFAC penalty (CBZ002, CBZ070) being raised at Presidential-diplomatic level as a material impediment to Zimbabwe's largest bank and by extension the financial sector's re-engagement with international correspondent banking relationships. The President also met Mozambican counterpart Filipe Nyusi to discuss food security, electricity shortages and accessing power from Cahora Bassa Dam, Cyclone Idai mitigation, and deepening economic cooperation in road, rail and fuel pipeline infrastructure development to transform Zimbabwe from landlocked to land-linked

headline: OK records spike in sales
content: OK Zimbabwe recorded sales growth of 167 percent during the first quarter of the current financial year to $398.9 million, maintaining gross profit margins at same levels as the year ended March 31, 2019, despite a macro-economic environment characterised by deterioration in the exchange rate, resultant escalation in prices, difficulties in accessing foreign currency to import goods, and erratic power supplies affecting operations such as in-store bakeries and forcing the group to rationalise opening hours and operate stores on costly generator power. The annual rate of inflation for June 2019 stood at 175 percent, up from 98 percent in May, though stability in prices was observed in July following the introduction of the local currency and accompanying policy measures. Despite increasing cost pressures, management remains optimistic of meeting profit targets for the financial year and will continue with stores rehabilitation programmes to enhance customer experience while new store openings remain a key focus, with the Karoi store already under construction. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, Econet Wireless, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned, though the article notes that mobile network Econet experienced a system blackout due to power outages affecting mobile money transactions.

headline: Delta’s first quarter volumes subdued
content: Delta Corporation recorded a subdued volume performance for the first quarter ended June 30, 2019 with lager beer volume declining 57 percent and sparkling beverages volume slumping 79 percent compared to the corresponding period last year, as the market continued to adjust to rising beverage pricing structures and market players adopted wide-ranging pricing models with retailers charging varying prices for the same products despite Delta's recommended retail prices. The sparkling beverages segment resumed full production after the company had closed three soft drink plants including the Graniteside plant in Harare earlier in 2019 after running out of critical raw materials, with Delta stating that fundamental changes in the economy arising from recent fiscal and monetary policies have significantly affected the business, the availability of foreign currency remains a challenge disrupting imported supplies into the value chain, and macroeconomic changes have led to a surge in inflation and a fast depreciating exchange rate resulting in erosion of disposable incomes and reduced consumer spending. Local sorghum beer volume grew 2 percent compared to Q1 2018, a segment that has been consistent despite difficulties in accessing imported packaging materials and services, while Natbrew Zambia reported positive volume trends following enhanced product formulation, though Delta expressed concerns about the supply of agricultural cereals arising from the El Nino-induced drought that adversely affected the 2018/19 rainfall season leading to minute cereal harvests and recent changes to marketing policies. This trading update captures the severe operating environment contemporaneous with Delta's May 2019 soft drinks price reductions to stimulate demand (DLTA007) and the broader economic crisis documented throughout your corpus including CBZ's US$424 million blocked funds (CBZ001) and Afdis's $45.5 million FX allocation backlog (AFDIS001).

headline: Zim’s assets want long-term capital: Mangudya
content: CBZ CEO Mudavanhu featured in investment promotion magazine","Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe Governor Dr John Mangudya has stated that Zimbabwe is endowed with quality assets requiring patient capital from investors with a medium to long-term view to exploit win-win opportunities, speaking in an interview with privately-owned local magazine 'Who's Who Zimbabwe' which features 200 local company executives talking up the local investment climate as part of private sector efforts complementing Government's drive to market the country as a safe and secure investment destination. CBZ Holdings chief executive officer Dr Blessing Mudavanhu is prominently featured among the executives interviewed alongside Zimplats CEO Mr Alex Mhembere, Toyota Zimbabwe managing director Mr Ian Howden, Fidelity Printers and Refiners general manager Mr Fradreck Kunaka, and Confederation of Zimbabwe Industries immediate past president Mr Sifelani Jabangwe. Executive editor and publisher Mr Confidence Musariri stated the initiative aims to counter the 90 percent negative and skewed information about Zimbabwe stemming from the past decade of economic strife, distributing the magazine largely outside the country through Zimbabwean embassies including Kenya and Namibia to link local businesses with foreign investors and potentially boost operational capacity for those in need of capital, with plans to produce an online version for continuous updating of the new age and new outlook in Zimbabwe. CBZ's inclusion in this high-profile investment promotion publication alongside the RBZ Governor and other industry captains demonstrates the bank's positioning as a leading voice in Zimbabwe's economic reform narrative and its chairman Holtzman's concurrent diplomatic advocacy campaign (CBZ016) for resetting US-Zimbabwe relations and attracting foreign direct investment.

headline: Man in soup over US$1m bank balance
content: High Court freezes CBZ account, orders Giles to explain US$1,048,553","Harare man Jairos Giles (41), managing director of Lapstar Logistics earning US$1,600 per month, has been issued a High Court order directing him to hand over a written statement to the police's Asset Forfeiture Unit explaining the source of US$1,048,553 that flowed through his CBZ Bank account between April and September 2018, with his bank account frozen and transactions barred, following an ex-parte application by the National Prosecuting Authority under Section 37B as read with Section 37H of the Presidential Powers (Temporary Measures) Amendment of the Money Laundering and Proceeds of Crime Act and Exchange Control Act regulations (Statutory Instrument 246 of 2018). Chief law officer Mr Chris Mutangadura, heading the Asset Forfeiture Unit, stated the State suspects Giles is involved in illegal foreign currency dealings on the parallel or black market and that the US$1,048,553 transaction amount substantially exceeds the statutory threshold of US$10,000, with investigations ongoing to ascertain if money and properties acquired in a short space of time are clean. Giles, a tenant paying US$400 monthly rent, previously worked for five years as chief dealer for Dragon Bureau de Change, and must also explain acquisition of three low-density immovable properties including stands 5892 and 5893 Crowhill, Borrowdale worth US$63,000, and four vehicles including Nissan Xtrail (AEN 4787), Nissan Tiida (AEW 0758), Toyota (777 897B) and Mercedes Benz (AEY 6720), with the State contending that legitimate income can only be proven by income tax declaration and that the wealth was ill-gotten. CBZ Bank is named solely as the financial institution holding the frozen account, with no allegation of wrongdoing against the bank presented.

headline: Econet Media shares up for grabs
content: Econet Media Limited, the Mauritius-incorporated subsidiary of Strive Masiyiwa's Econet Group, put up for sale its shares in satellite broadcast, free-to-air television and digital media distribution businesses across 13 countries following insolvency, having racked up over US$130 million in external liabilities and being placed under voluntary administration after failing to pay suppliers . The group, which traded under the brands Kwese TV or Econet Media, operates in South Africa, Botswana, Nigeria, Ghana, Dubai, Kenya, Malawi, Lesotho, Rwanda, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia and Mauritius, with Ernst and Young's Paul Gerald Lincoln appointed as licensed insolvency practitioner to manage the process . The closure followed Econet's August 2019 announcement discontinuing Kwese TV Satellite Service, with group CEO Douglas Mboweni stating: 'The third-party content providers on whose content we rely, require payment in foreign currency. With the prevailing economic conditions in Zimbabwe, and the current business operating environment - characterised by an acute shortage of foreign currency - sustaining Kwese and Kwese Satellite Service was no longer viable' . The Kwese brand had launched two years prior competing with MultiChoice's DStv across Africa, but faced content price inflation, fiercely competitive PayTV markets, and the economic impact of Zimbabwe's June 2019 demonetisation which exacerbated foreign currency shortages . The Econet Group emphasised that the rest of its businesses, including Econet Wireless Zimbabwe, continue to operate normally as separate legal entities with their own management teams and boards . Subsequent reports confirmed retrenched Kwese staff across 13 countries had received only 30-50 percent of promised packages by December 2019, with the Econet Group sourcing funds internally for payments while awaiting asset sale proceeds

headline: Stock Market weakness persist
content: CBZ remains flat at 55c","The Zimbabwe Stock Exchange weakness persisted on August 27, 2019 as top cap counters dragged indices down with the ZSE Top 10 Index backtracking 3.82 percent to 149.46, the ZSE All Share Index retreating 2.63 percent to 167.26 points, the Industrials Index declining 2.67 percent to 556.3 points, and total market value falling 2.66 percent to $21.8 billion on losses in big cap counters. Simbisa, Delta, Old Mutual, Innscor and Econet were the top five value contributors for the session, with Simbisa also the biggest casualty falling 13.93 percent to 60.04c, BAT down 11.79 percent to $30, Axia down 8.96 percent to 42.79c, Cassava down 4.9 percent to $1.22, Powerspeed down 4.86 percent to 32.87c, Delta down 2.99 percent to $3.10, Econet down 3.26 percent to $1.20, Innscor down 3.14 percent to $1.88, and Old Mutual down 3.27 percent to $17.89. CBZ Holdings remained unchanged at 55 cents alongside Zimpapers (8c), Proplastics (38c) and NMB (28c). Gains were recorded in Willdale (+8.7% to 2.50c), TSL (+7.5% to 75.25c), Dawn (+3.9% to 4.8c), ZBFH (+3.23% to 62c) and Mashonaland Holdings (+2.09% to 4.40c), with Padenga advancing 0.3 percent to $1.65 after shareholders approved proposed acquisition of a majority stake in Dallaglio Investments. Nearly 21 million shares worth $14.4 million exchanged hands, a 228 percent jump from prior session.

headline: First seed grading plant for Glendale
content: Seed Co Ltd's biggest seed producer Mr Len Smit, a multi-award winning farmer and first beneficiary of the Seed Co Ltd Grower Transformation Initiative (GTI)—a scheme developed to capacitate seed producers with irrigation and farm and seed handling equipment—is establishing the first seed grading plant in Glendale, Mashonaland Central Province. Smit, who in particular seasons produces 200ha seed maize, 175ha seed soya and 200ha seed wheat all under irrigation as a producer of seed soyabeans, seed wheat and parent/basic seed, stated that the installation of seed grading plants and driers across all provinces will ensure the growth of commercial agriculture by improving seed production and enabling quick delivery of grains from field to market. The on-farm processing plant will clean and grade seed before delivery for treatment, with driers ensuring grains are dried on time for market and extending the farming season. Smit indicated that once necessary paperwork with the lands office is completed, operations are expected to commence by October 2019 with at least 500 employees at the factory opening. Lands, Agriculture, Water, Climate and Rural Resettlement Deputy Minister Douglas Karoro expressed Government's readiness to support such initiatives, stating they enhance production in the commercial sector and go a long way in ensuring seed quality. This investment follows Seed Co's extensive agricultural development portfolio including the Stapleford vegetable trial station (SEED004) and the company's role in Command Agriculture and Presidential Inputs Scheme as documented in PAC reports (citation:1).

headline: BAT in 20pc volumes drop
content: "BAT Zimbabwe recorded a 20 percent decline in sales volumes for the half year ended June 30, 2019, with value for money brands decreasing 21 percent and imported brand Dunhill plunging 87 percent due to sinking consumer disposable incomes and the company's inability to import as duties are required in foreign currency for a brand that is then sold in local dollars, according to financial director Leslie Malunga at an analysts and media briefing. Foreign currency and electricity shortages critically compromised efficiency and overall production capacity for the manufacturing sector. Despite the volume decline, revenue increased 48 percent to $9.6 million driven by price increases targeted at containing cost increases, with gross profit margins increasing 61 percent to $8.9 million boosted by raw materials sourced at lower prices. Inflationary pressures weighed on profitability with marketing costs increasing 54 percent to $3.9 million and administrative expenses surging 117 percent driven by once-off restructuring costs and economic inflationary pressures. Net profit at $8.5 million was 15 percent up from prior year comparative. The company did not declare a dividend to allow reinvestment in operations. Acting managing director Stephen Nyabadza expressed confidence that the company's strategy remained appropriate to the operating environment. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, Econet Wireless, Seed Co, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: CBZ commits to fund Command Agriculture
content: "Lands, Agriculture, Water, Climate and Rural Resettlement Deputy Minister Vangelis Peter Haritatos has confirmed that CBZ Holdings has agreed in principle to fund Command Agriculture as Government intensifies preparations for the 2019/20 summer cropping season, with $2.8 billion released by the Ministry of Finance and Economic Development for the programme currently being distributed and procurement of inputs underway. Deputy Minister Haritatos stated that CBZ has come on board and will help take Command Agriculture to greater heights, expressing excitement that the financial institution is now in the mix and farmers will be better supported. He emphasized the need for expedited farming preparations following weather experts' prediction of normal rains with a bias towards below normal throughout the 2019/20 season, urging that inputs be distributed by end of September or latest October, with November 15 targeted as planting day. The Deputy Minister also addressed the ongoing process of issuing 99-year leases to farmers as a critical security of tenure measure to improve productivity, encouraging all farmers to submit applications and warning that sitting on farms without even an offer letter is unwise and requires regularization. This commitment precedes the October 4, 2019 ministerial policy mandate (CBZ033) where Minister Perrance Shiri formally designated CBZ among four partner banks with 10.5% interest, 270-day tenure, and Government guarantees, and the October 3, 2019 launch of CBZ Agro-Yield (CBZ035) as the dedicated agricultural finance division to house the partnership."

headline: 12m shares in ZSE trade
content: 12M SHARES WORTH $7 MILLION TRADED ON ZSE FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 13, 2019 WITH DELTA, OLD MUTUAL, INNSCOR, FBC AND ECONET AS TOP VALUE DRIVERS — SIMBISA HEADLINED RISERS WITH 12 PERCENT GAIN TO 65 CENTS, OLD MUTUAL PUT ON 6.7 PERCENT TO $25, WHILE OK ZIMBABWE WAS THE BIGGEST CASUALTY EASING 8 PERCENT TO 32 CENTS DESPITE BEING TOP VOLUME DRIVER WITH 1.4 MILLION SHARES — DELTA, ECONET AND SEED CO INTERNATIONAL SUFFERED MARGINAL LOSSES, WITH THE TWO ACTIVE RESOURCES COUNTERS REMAINING UNCHANGED

headline: CBZ launches Agro-Yield
content: CBZ Holdings Limited has launched CBZ Agro-Yield, a new agricultural finance division established to house the bank's partnership with Government for financing maize and soya bean farming under the Command Agriculture programme for the 2019-20 season. The initiative aims to curb the ever-growing import bill for agricultural produce, with locally produced beans reducing demand for imported Crude Degummed Soya Bean Oil and lessening the amount of foreign currency used for purchasing raw materials. The current scheme is restricted to maize and soya farmers, with those having irrigation facilities prioritised. Deputy Minister of Lands, Agriculture, Water, Climate and Rural Settlement Vangelis Haritatos stated at a soya bean public seminar that Government was looking forward to more private sector cooperation to achieve desired soyabean yield. This foundational announcement precedes the October 4, 2019 ministerial policy mandate (CBZ033) where Minister Perrance Shiri formally designated CBZ among four partner banks with 10.5% interest, 270-day tenure, and Government guarantees, and the November 2019 operational rollout (CBZ017) registering 300+ farmers in Matabeleland South.

headline: BAT appoints MD
content: Kimesh Naidoo takes over","British American Tobacco Zimbabwe Holdings Limited has appointed Mr Kimesh Naidoo as managing director effective October 22, 2019, succeeding Clara Mlambo who resigned after a 21-year career with the company. Company secretary Pauline Kadembo advised shareholders in a notice that Naidoo, a qualified Chartered Accountant with the South Africa Institute of Chartered Accountants, brings over 11 years' experience in cross functional management, financial management, audit, sales and distribution in the fast-moving consumer goods industry. Given his experience, Naidoo is expected to maintain a hard-line stance in lobbying for policies addressing the company's needs. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, Econet Wireless, Seed Co, Hippo Valley Estates, Dairiboard Zimbabwe, CAFCA Limited, OK Zimbabwe, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: Dr Dish, Econet case takes new twist
content: High Court Justice Edith Mushore delivered her reasons for judgment granting Dr Dish an Anton Pillar order to search premises belonging to Cassava Smartech and Econet Wireless Zimbabwe, ruling that Dr Dish established a prima facie basis for the intervention and that all points required for the Anton Pillar award to succeed were present in the case. Dr Dish, which is owed over $6.8 million by Econet Media as part of their revenue sharing agreement when Kwese TV was launched, won its case early in 2019. Justice Mushore noted that 'the accounts detailing the subscriber fully paid up revenue form the basis of applicant's potential claim for its five percent fee are in respondents' custody' and that 'it is also not far-fetched for the applicant to entertain a fear that the respondents may hide the documentation.' When Dr Dish and the Sheriff of the High Court executed the order at Cassava's Borrowdale offices, the sheriff was stopped and asked to leave mid-way through the search on EcoCash records, after which the sheriff reported a case of obstruction of justice. Econet argued that the records being perused were EcoCash records and not Cassava records, and that EcoCash was separate and excluded from the court order, but Justice Mushore ruled that EcoCash as a 100 percent owned subsidiary of Cassava Smartech could not be separated from its parent. Econet sought the court's reasons to enable it to prosecute an appeal in the Supreme Court. The underlying dispute centres on Econet's claim that only 38 Kwese TV subscribers existed in Zimbabwe during the subsistence of the multimillion-dollar deal, resulting in a paltry US$58,000 owed to Dr Dish. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned."

headline: Delta sales volumes on a decline
content: Delta Corporation reported severe volume declines across main segments and subsidiaries for the second quarter and half year ended September 30, 2019, driven by monetary policy changes, a surge in inflation, fast depreciating exchange rate, low disposable incomes, compressed consumer spending, and shortages of potable water, electricity, fuel and foreign currency, with the company implementing modest but frequent price increases in response to inflationary pressures whilst considering consumer affordability. Lager beer volume declined 40 percent for the quarter and 48 percent for the six months compared to the same period last year, with pricing moderated to maintain affordability given prevailing economic challenges. Sorghum beer volume in Zimbabwe fell 29 percent for the quarter and 15 percent for the six months due to rises in prices of maize, sorghum and packaging materials beyond disposable incomes, forcing price increases that resulted in customers switching from the category. Sparkling beverages volumes fell 36 percent for the quarter and 56 percent for the six months, constrained by raw material supply challenges given the category's high import content. Associate company African Distillers Limited recorded a soft volume out-turn due to difficulties in accessing foreign currency, reactively launching products with lower foreign currency content. Associate Schweppes Holdings recorded a 33 percent beverage volumes fall for the half year on ailing consumer demand and difficulty in accessing imported raw materials. Natbrew Zambia volumes declined 13 percent partly due to higher pricing on steep maize price increases and Kwacha depreciation, with capacity and power supply further constraining product supply. The company stated challenges in sourcing imported raw materials and services persist due to mixed performance of the interbank market and difficulties accessing materials as foreign suppliers remain jittery on account of overdue payables. This trading update captures the severe operating environment documented in your corpus throughout 2019, contemporaneous with CBZ's US$424 million blocked funds disclosure (CBZ001), Afdis's $45.5 million FX allocation backlog (AFDIS001), and the Paynet dispute where banks including CBZ could not settle US$470,000 contractual debt in USD (CBZ029).

headline: BREAKING: Guard shoots self at Econet Shop
content: A Guard Alert security detail identified only as Nyamadzawo shot himself once in the head at an Econet Wireless Shop at the intersection of First Street and George Silundika Avenue at approximately 5pm on November 26, 2019, in unclear circumstances. Sources indicated the security guard was questioned by Econet officials over some cellphones that recently went missing and was facing financial problems. According to witnesses, the security guard shot himself in a toilet on the first floor of the shop, causing several people inside the shop and outside vendors to scamper for cover. Police attended the scene, and national police spokesperson Assistant Commissioner Paul Nyathi stated he was still checking details of the case. This incident has no material financial or operational implications for Econet Wireless Zimbabwe, and no CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned

headline: President appoints two perm secretaries
content: CBZ technical director Dr John Basera appointed Permanent Secretary","President Mnangagwa has appointed renowned agronomist and CBZ technical director Dr John Basera as the new Permanent Secretary in the Ministry of Lands, Agriculture, Water and Rural Resettlement effective January 1, 2020, replacing long-serving senior civil servant Mr Ringson Chitsiko, according to an announcement by Chief Secretary to the President and Cabinet Dr Misheck Sibanda. Dr Basera holds a degree in agronomy, a Masters in Business Administration and a PhD in agribusiness, with extensive experience in agriculture focusing on agronomy, agricultural research extension services, agricultural value chain management, agricultural strategy, finance and marketing across Zimbabwe, Zambia, Mozambique, Malawi and Namibia, having worked in the private sector including at Seed Co and currently as technical director in charge of the agriculture portfolio at CBZ. His appointment comes at a time when Government is focusing on revitalising agriculture as a critical component of national efforts to realise Vision 2030. This appointment precedes Dr Basera's subsequent elevation to Permanent Secretary for Local Government and Public Works in October 2023, where he led the Call2Action: No Compromise to Service Delivery Blueprint, and his prior role as CBZ Agro-Yield Managing Director before joining the public service in November 2019 (CBZ061). Engineer Silibaziso Chizwina was simultaneously appointed Permanent Secretary for the newly-created Ministry of National Housing and Social Amenities with immediate effect, bringing experience from Noczim, NSSA, Ministry of Education and Udcorp.

headline: Delta in sharp volumes decline
content: Delta Corporation reported massive volume declines across all segments for the quarter ended September 30, 2019 as consumers cut spending on less basic commodities due to reduced disposable incomes following the liberalisation of the exchange rate in February 2019 and rocketing inflation, which cooled to 17.5 percent month-on-month in November from 38.75 percent in October—the lowest rate in five months—though independent analysts estimated year-on-year inflation at 467 percent in November. Lager beer volumes declined 48 percent compared to the same period last year, with pricing moderated to maintain affordability while premium category contribution was held by Zambezi Lager. Sorghum beer volumes declined 15 percent as prices of major inputs including maize and imported packaging materials rose ahead of disposable incomes, pressuring prices and leading consumers to switch to more affordable brands and packs within the category, with Chibuku Super and Shake Shake as dominant packs though product supply constrained by capacity and power supply disruptions. Sparkling beverages volumes declined 56 percent, recovering in the last quarter on improved product supply and moderated retail pricing, though raw material supply—largely imported—remains a challenge. Associate company African Distillers Limited recorded a soft volume out-turn at 41 percent below prior year due to limitations in accessing foreign currency, directly corroborating Afdis's contemporaneous disclosure of $45.5 million awaiting foreign currency allocation at banks (AFDIS001, March 2020). Associate Schweppes Holdings Africa experienced reduced volume performance at 33 percent below prior year due to limited foreign currency supply for packaging material and reduced demand from higher pricing. Natbrew Zambia volumes declined 20 percent from last year's record due to higher pricing on steep maize price increases and Kwacha depreciation. Delta's revenue slightly dropped 2 percent to $1.5 billion while earnings before interest and tax increased 53 percent to $464 million driven by replacement cost pricing in response to inflationary pressures, with overheads under control despite foreign exchange-induced cost escalations, cash balance of $296 million, and foreign currency exposure of US$72 million remaining a concern as the economy yet to settle from the multi-currency transition and accompanying policy measures under the Government Stabilisation Programme.

headline: Econet suffers $1,9bn exchange losses
content: Econet Wireless Zimbabwe incurred ZWL$1.9 billion in foreign exchange losses for the half year ended August 31, 2019 due to accelerated depreciation of the local currency and outstanding foreign currency obligations, according to chairman Dr James Myers in a statement accompanying the group's financial results. Despite the exchange losses, the group's net foreign currency position remained positive due to its investment in Liquid Telecoms amounting to US$135 million at the time of transaction, which has been accounted for with a fair value adjustment of ZWL$1.1 billion processed through the statement of other comprehensive income. All US dollar amortising debt was extinguished using funds raised through a Rights Offer in 2017, with existing obligations relating to vendor credit secured against equipment provided by vendors. Revenue for the half year amounted to ZWL$1.3 billion, with management implementing appropriate cost efficiency strategies in light of the deteriorating economic environment. The group owns approximately 20 percent of Cassava Smartech Zimbabwe Limited, separately listed on the ZSE, with equity accounted earnings of ZWL$22.7 million reported and current valuation of ZWL$777 million as at August 31, 2019 representing embedded value. Dr Myers stated the group remains confident in the prospects of the business and its capability to remain resilient in the wake of challenges, emphasising the robust business model has demonstrated resilience over time and expressing hope that policy thrust from authorities will be anchored on creating a sustainable business environment. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

headline: BAT Zim’s volumes dip
content: BAT Zimbabwe's overall volumes declined 18 percent for the nine months ended September 30, 2019, with Dunhill premium brand plunging 92 percent due to the company's constrained capacity to import the brand as excise duty is payable in foreign currency which has been difficult for most companies to access, following the shift from a single specific to a mixed excise regime system. Newbury and Kingsgate brands had a combined decline of 3 percent, value-for-money brands Everest and Madison declined 17 percent cumulatively, and Ascot brand weighed on low-value-for-money brands which experienced a 27 percent dip. Despite the sales volumes decline, the group recorded positive revenue growth and is in line to post a profit. Trading update results were delayed due to limited time and processes required in terms of International Accounting Standard 29 – Financial Reporting in Hyper-inflationary Economies. Morgan & Co analysts view the group's stock price as 'expensive', noting BAT Zimbabwe trades on a prospective PE multiple of 54.3x against a respective PE average of other listed BAT counters of 12.5x, and a dividend yield of 3.59 percent significantly falls short compared to a consolidated global BAT average yield of 7.95 percent. Persistent foreign currency shortages continue to limit BAT Zimbabwe's ability to supply the Dunhill brand and source packaging materials, while falling disposable incomes have hampered demand for all its brands. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, Econet Wireless, Seed Co, Hippo Valley Estates, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

Delta's revenue slightly dropped 2 percent to $1.5 billion while earnings before interest and tax increased 53 percent to $464 million driven by replacement cost pricing in response to inflationary pressures, with overheads under control despite foreign exchange-induced cost escalations, cash balance of $296 million, and foreign currency exposure of US$72 million remaining a concern as the economy yet to settle from the multi-currency transition and accompanying policy measures under the Government Stabilisation Programme.

headline: Small business stories that inspired in 2019
content: The Herald's year-end SME round-up highlighted CBZ Holdings' Youth Entrepreneurship Programme (YEP) as one of the deliberate financial sector initiatives intentionally targeting SME products and youth start-ups, noting that YEP has 'assisted a sizable number of youth SME start-ups' alongside Steward Bank's Pod programme for building business capacity for SMEs and ZB Bank's partnership with Kukura Business Accelerator to enhance children's entrepreneurship. The article also commended Financial Securities Exchange Limited's Growth Enterprises Market Portal enabling qualifying SMEs to raise capital on the stock exchange, and acknowledged that while start-up funds for innovative products remain very low, the financial sector is now leaning towards designing and offering products meant for SMEs. This recognition follows CBZ's March 2019 announcement that nearly 3,000 youths had benefited from YEP over three years, with CEO Dr Blessing Mudavanhu stating the initiative fits into the RBZ's financial inclusion strategy and provides a platform for young people to create jobs rather than hunt for jobs (CBZ053), and precedes the bank's December 2024 announcement of new financial inclusion initiatives targeting the informal sector under Group CEO Lawrence Nyazema (CBZ080)

headline: ZSE begins year on high note
content: The Zimbabwe Stock Exchange sustained gains for the second consecutive week in January 2020, with total value of trades increasing 6 percent to $22.2 million and weekly volumes rising 29.9 percent to 14.3 million shares. The ZSE All Share Index gained 0.83 percent to 234 points, with total market value closing at $30.2 billion. Big cap counters drove performance: Cassava contributed 39 percent of total volumes and 38 percent of value, Delta contributed 21 percent of value, Econet accounted for 10 percent of volumes, and Old Mutual and Innscor each contributed 9 percent to total value. CAFCA Limited featured among the week's top risers, advancing 12 percent to close at $2, alongside Powerspeed (+20%), Zimpapers (+16%), FCB (+11%), OK Zimbabwe (+8%), Axia (+7%), TSL (+5%) and ZB (+2%). On the downside, CBZ Holdings retreated 1 percent to 69 cents, joining decliners including Willdale (-29%), Edgars (-14%), FML (-10%), NMB (-9%), RioZim (-3%), Padenga (-1%), ZPI (-0.7%), Simbisa (-0.7%) and Delta (-0.2%)

headline: Econet suffers volume decline
content: Econet Wireless Zimbabwe recorded a nightmarish performance for the third quarter ended October 31, 2019 with traffic volumes declining across all segments—voice traffic down 8 percent, data traffic down 30 percent, and SMS traffic down 35 percent from the previous quarter—following headline tariff adjustments in August 2019 and October 2019, with the mobile network operator customising consumer packages to maintain affordability in light of depressed consumer disposable incomes whilst pricing within the confines of responsible business practice. The business stated that tariffs continue to lag behind inflation and given the rapid local currency depreciation since February 2019, tariffs are now at 'sub-economic levels', threatening investment outlay critical for a technology-driven company and the viability of business considering high operating costs, with erratic grid power supply and escalating fuel costs significantly impacting base station running costs. Econet and other industry players will continue to engage with POTRAZ in an effort to return to a tariff regime that ensures continued sector viability and maintenance of quality of service standards. Year-on-year performance for Q3 2020 against Q3 2019 was positive with voice traffic up 20 percent, data up 26 percent and SMS up 7 percent, reflective of strong market demand, though depreciation of the local currency is expected to negatively impact the group's financial performance going forward. No CBZ Holdings, African Distillers Limited, Delta Corporation, or any other ZSE-listed company previously documented in this corpus is mentioned.

The ZSE All Share Index gained 0.83 percent to 234 points, with total market value closing at $30.2 billion.
