headline: Corporate Action Summary (August 2023)"
content: "Appointments: Econet appointed Tatenda Alice Ngowe as Group Company Secretary effective August 1, 2023. Ngowe has been with the Econet Group for 10 years, with experience in finance, strategy, corporate governance, and investor relations. Press Releases: Econet held an EGM on August 7, 2023, where shareholders authorized a renounceable rights offer of 401,586,371 new shares in the ratio of 17.122 new shares for every 100 shares held. The rights offer may be taken up in USD or via Econet Debentures at US$0.06252 per debenture. Econet also announced its 25th AGM will be held virtually on August 31, 2023. (Note: Ecocash Holdings, Caledonia, and GetBucks announcements are referenced but not detailed).

headline: CBZ targets merging operations with FMHL
content: CBZ Holdings chief executive officer Mr Blessing Mudavanhu confirmed that the group's strategic thrust following the acquisition of a 36 percent stake in First Mutual Holdings Limited is to move towards merging the operations of CBZ Holdings and First Mutual Holdings to achieve the scale needed to compete against larger corporates in Zimbabwe and the region, describing the acquisition as a significant milestone in the group's pursuit of its long-term growth strategy. Mudavanhu stated that FMHL's business model presents a natural fit with CBZHL as it brings a giant leap into the insurance space, with FMHL's SADC regional operations providing CBZH subsidiaries a stepping stone for expansion, and the exploitation of synergies between the two businesses should unlock more value for the combined entity, enhancing insurance and property businesses and widening product offering to the significant client base, presenting 'a well-oiled bancassurance model to the Zimbabwe market.' The combined businesses will expand trading abilities across geographies, permitting extension of product management and distribution capabilities, improving customer product offerings, and better absorbing market shocks through a deeper and further diversified capital base, with Mudavanhu stating the transaction will add significant shareholder value to both entities and to the country by ensuring the economic mass acquired will be used to underwrite larger transactions. FMHL chief executive Mr Douglas Hoto stated the transaction was largely informed by the need for Zimbabwe to have a financial institution consistent with national interests and the national development agenda, building capacity to finance and secure investors for projects, converting less structured economic possibilities in the informal sector into an inclusive new vision creating an upper middle-class economy. NSSA acting general manager Dr Charles Shava noted the transaction marks the culmination of a journey starting in 2020 when NSSA embarked on an investment refocus strategy involving consolidation and optimization of investments to unlock value for members, leaving NSSA as the single biggest shareholder in both FMHL and CBZ, describing it as 'one of our best stock market decisions in a long time.' This acquisition follows CBZ's January 2022 announcement of the share purchase agreement with NSSA (CBZ101) and precedes the December 2023 strategic leadership transition where Mudavanhu retired and Marc Holtzman stepped down as chairman, with Luxon Zembe appointed acting chairman to superintend the merger (CBZ079)

headline: Chibuku Road to Fame competitions begin
content: Delta Beverages has announced that the 2023 Chibuku Road to Fame music competitions will commence on September 9, 2023 and run through to October 14, 2023 under the theme 'Imba Tinzwe, Cula Sizwe Superstar,' with provincial finals across all 10 provinces culminating in national finals in Harare. Delta Corporation marketing director Irimayi Muzorewa stated the talent identification platform allows upcoming artists to step into stardom as they begin their musical journey, with Delta working closely with the National Arts Council of Zimbabwe in identifying and nurturing artists as they launch their musical careers. Contestants stand to win monetary prizes at both provincial and national levels including a recording contract for the national final winner, with first prize US$15,000, second prize US$10,000, and third prize US$7,000. In a bid to promote the culture of music and dance, this year's edition includes new genres Zimdancehall and hip hop alongside traditional genres, as the brand continues to leverage music and dance to deliver its core purpose of nourishing the bonds of brotherhood amongst consumers who have a strong appreciation for music. Last year's competition was won by Mashonaland West-based afro-fusion and jazz contemporary outfit Grade 2D, who pocketed US$15,000 at the Glamis Arena ceremony, with second place The Grooves from Midlands receiving US$10,000 and third place Shantani Musical Band from Matabeleland North awarded US$7,000. The 2022 event coincided with Delta Corporation's 60th anniversary celebration of the Chibuku brand. This sponsorship follows Delta's extensive arts and culture portfolio including the Chibuku Neshamwari Traditional Dance Festival (DLTA020), which was revived in 2022 after a three-year COVID-19 hiatus.

headline: ZimTrade launches Horticulture Export Masterclass
content: CBZ partners to enlist smallholder farmers into mainstream export business","ZimTrade in partnership with PUM, CBZ and Chainn launched the Horticulture Export Masterclass (HEM2023) on September 6, 2023 as the national trade development and promotion body aims to enlist smallholder farmers and bring them into mainstream export business, upgrading from The Best Model Farm Project which ran between 2019-2022 with three medium-sized farms from Mashonaland East, Mashonaland West and Midlands Provinces successfully growing peas for export and diversifying into blueberries, avocadoes, sweet potatoes and citrus production. The masterclass seeks to improve mid-sized farmers' export skills particularly those intending to export into the European Union market which imports US$118.7 billion worth of horticulture produce accounting for over 45 percent of global horticultural trade, with the Netherlands as a significant importer, while also availing key markets including the Middle East, South East Asia and regional destinations. ZimTrade Director of Operations Mr Similo Nkala stated the programme aligns with the national horticulture recovery and growth plan targeting US$300 million in horticulture exports by 2030 under the National Development Strategy 1 (2021-2025), with Treasury having allocated US$30 million horticulture export revolving fund from IMF Special Drawing Rights in September 2022 to capacitate local farmers in boosting production and tackling challenges related to unavailability of appropriately structured financing for short to long-term expenditures. The Horticultural Development Council is advancing production of blueberries, citrus, coffee and flowers, with plans to secure an additional 4,000 hectares for blueberry production estimated at US$140 million to develop, and to establish 1,000 hectares of coffee in the Eastern Highlands supporting 1,300 small and medium-scale coffee producers to produce circa 2,220 tonnes annually with gross value over US$11 million. CBZ's partnership in this export-oriented agricultural initiative demonstrates continued strategic alignment with Government's horticulture export drive and follows the bank's established agricultural financing mandate under Command Agriculture/NEAPS, the US$40 million Afreximbank export facility, and chairman Holtzman's FDI conduit strategy.

headline: Econet Wireless shines at National Exceptional Marketing Awards
content: "Econet Wireless Zimbabwe dominated the National Exceptional Marketing Awards (hosted by Marketers Association of Zimbabwe), winning five categories: Best Radio Advert, Best Print Advert ('Hallo 25' anniversary), Best Social Media & Digital Marketing Campaigns, Best Interactive Marketing & Gaming Experience, and Best Promotional Campaign. Individual accolades: Group CEO Dr Douglas Mboweni (Marketing Oriented CEO Award), GM Marketing Tariro Muchena (Champion Marketer - Executive), and Brand Manager Kudzai Muneno (Brand Manager of the Year). MAZ Executive Secretary Gillian Rusike noted the awards recognize brands that have 'managed to stand out' amid evolving marketing paradigms. Other winners included Zimnat, Nyaradzo, OK Zimbabwe, Stanbic, Delta, and FBC Holdings. The recognition validates Econet's marketing excellence and brand strength, reinforcing its market leadership and customer connection. For investors, this signals strong brand equity and management capability."

headline: "Exchange Losses Impede Dairibord's Profitability"
content: "Dairibord reported a loss after tax of ZWL4 billion for H1 2023, a 157% decline from a ZWL7 billion profit in H1 2022. The company incurred foreign exchange losses totalling ZWL27.49 billion, with foreign exchange losses amounting to ZWL42.55 billion. The Zimbabwe dollar depreciated 88% against the USD during the period (from ZWL776 to ZWL6326). Despite losses, raw milk utilization increased 16% to 14.226 million litres, with Dairibord accounting for 34% of total processor intake. Total sales volumes grew 9%, with 64% of volumes sold in foreign currency (up from 39%). Liquid Milk volumes grew 6%, Beverages grew 16% (due to capital investments), but Foods declined 23% due to input supply inconsistencies and depressed ice cream demand.

headline: Top acts for Chibuku Road to Fame finale
content: "Delta Corporation's Chibuku Road to Fame finale is set for October 2023 at Takashinga Cricket Club, Highfields, featuring 10 finalists alongside established acts (Alick Macheso, Tocky Vibes, Feli Nandi, Chillspot Family, Jah Master, Ndunge Yut, Pumacol, Bazooker, Grade 2D Band). Marketing Director Irimayi Muzorewa confirmed provincial competitions ran Sept 9-Oct 2023, with free entry to the finale. The initiative empowers aspiring artists, with winners receiving US$15,000 (first), US$10,000 (second), and US$7,000 (third). Grade 2D Band (2022 winners) exemplify the programme's impact, having emerged from obscurity to national recognition. The event builds on Delta's long-standing music sponsorship, including Chibuku Neshamwari traditional dance festival [citation:DLTA097] and aligns with the brand's 60+ year heritage of community engagement. For investors, this represents strategic investment in brand relevance, youth engagement, and cultural connection.

headline: Gweru 911 Band wins Chibuku Road To Fame finals
content: "Midlands-based Afro-fusion band 911 Band won the Chibuku Road To Fame 2023 national finals at Takashinga Cricket Club, Highfield, Harare, beating nine provincial winners. Prize money: US$15,000 (first), US$10,000 (second – High Five Band, Mash Central), US$7,500 (third – Kweseka Band, Mat North). 911 Band, formed two years ago, rose from provincial to national champions. Manager Mathew Mabengo thanked Delta Beverages for 'uplifting the arts sector' and providing 'a chance to showcase talent.' Delta Corporate Affairs Executive Patricia Murambinda stated: 'As Delta beverages, it's our responsibility to pay back to the communities we are working under. We are not doing this for prestige, but we are doing this as part of our corporate social responsibility.' Since inception in 2001, the competition has launched artists including Valembe Stars, Bopoma Stars, Club Shanga, Romeo Gasa, and Terry and the Bliss. The event featured performances by Alick Macheso, Feli Nandi, and Chillspot Records. Feli Nandi paid tribute to late artists Garry Mapanzure and Soul Jah Love."

headline: Afdis applauds Govt over economic measures
content: Afdis applauds Govt over economic measures; volumes +11%, revenue +156%","African Distillers Limited chairman Mr Matlhogonolo Valela has praised Government's efforts to stabilise the economy, expressing optimism that the falling inflation trajectory and exchange rate stability can be sustained following policy measures implemented in response to the free-falling exchange rate and high inflation in Q1 2023, including allowing 100 percent retention of domestic foreign currency earnings, transfer of external payment obligations from RBZ to Treasury, refining the foreign exchange auction system, lifting restrictions on importation of basic goods, promoting domestic currency use by Government agencies, introducing gold coins and gold-backed digital tokens for alternative investment and excess liquidity management, and directing taxpayers to settle 50 percent of obligations in foreign currency from June 2023 QPD while hiking interest rates to discourage speculative borrowing. In the firm's half-year financial results to September 30, 2023, Afdis recorded 11 percent overall volume growth against the prior year comparative period, benefitting from good product availability across all key brands, intensified product distribution and brand innovation, with spirits segment growing 8 percent, wines surging 7 percent driven by increased market penetration in Q2, and Ready to Drink segment registering 14 percent growth despite competition from lower-priced smuggled imports. Group revenue increased 156 percent to $134 billion in inflation-adjusted terms, operating income increased to $28.3 billion, and the group declared an interim dividend of US$0.0030 per share amounting to US$355,882. Valela noted consumer demand was negatively impacted by currency-induced pricing distortions persisting in the formal retail channel, while informal traders benefited from stable and competitive pricing dominated by the US dollar and strong demand linked to 2023 elections-related spending, with management continuing to focus on supplying full product range, identifying growth opportunities, defending market share and improving profitability through product innovation, production efficiencies and cost control."

headline: OK Zimbabwe sees lukewarm half-year volume outturn
content: OK Zimbabwe Limited reported significantly weak first-half volumes for FY2024, with volumes declining 21.6% in Q1 and worsening to 23.9% in Q2, resulting in a year-to-date decline of 22.6%. The formal retail sector faced weak consumer demand due to currency volatility and inflation impacting disposable incomes. The group operated below breakeven point as consumers shifted to informal traders offering parallel market rates. Challenges included high interest rates, limited funding access, increased operational costs (rentals, electricity, labour), supplier term reductions causing stockouts, and competition from informal traders. Despite this, OK invested in new pharmacies, acquired Food Lovers, and undertook store refurbishments. CEO Maxen Karombo noted the rapid informalisation of the retail channel. The company welcomed the extension of the multicurrency regime to 2030 and RBZ recommendations to remove the 10% exchange rate limit on formal retailers and eliminate IMTT on card transactions. The group plans strategic partnerships with Shoprite South Africa, money transfer agencies, and insurers for H2 recovery.

headline: OK half-year revenue in 60pc jump
content: OK Zimbabwe reported revenue growth of 60.38 percent to $727.9 billion in inflation-adjusted terms for the half year ended September 30, 2023 (473.46 percent historical increase to $541.3 billion), despite volumes declining 22.6 percent compared to the same period last year. Chairman Herbert Nkala stated the reporting period opened with RBZ Auction Rate at $929.86 and closed at $5,466.74, with rapid local currency depreciation causing sharp price increases and depressed consumer affordability. The continued mandatory use of the official exchange rate with capped 10 percent margin for formal retail caused significant volume loss to the informal sector enjoying more exchange rate flexibility, with suppliers resorting to shortened trading terms to hedge against exchange rate losses, resulting in high incidence of stock-outs. Operating costs increased 886.83 percent in historical terms driven by utilities and backup power expenses, transport and delivery, maintenance expenses, and labour costs. Exchange losses on foreign-denominated liabilities and leases amounted to $32.4 billion, negatively impacting profitability. Profit after tax reached $21.2 billion compared to $13.9 billion in 2022, though historical cost terms showed net loss of $9.2 billion versus $2.2 billion in 2022. Post reporting period, the group began liquidating foreign currency-denominated liabilities and renegotiating foreign currency-based leases to reduce future exchange rate losses. Capital expenditure grew to $16.8 billion (from $7.4 billion last year), channelled towards new Bon Marché Marondera store and new Alowell Pharmacy outlets now operational instore at selected branches. The group welcomed MPC recommendation to remove the 10 percent margin cap on instore exchange rate and removal of Intermediated Money Transfer tax on card transactions.

headline: EXCLUSIVE: CBZ in major strategic shake up
content: Zembe appointed acting chairman to lead ZB merger, Nyazema acting CEO","CBZ Holdings has announced a major strategic leadership transition effective December 31, 2023, with renowned banker and strategist Mr Luxon Zembe appointed acting chairman to superintend the proposed merger with ZB Financial Holdings, taking over from Mr Marc Holtzman who has tendered his retirement after four years of service during which he steered the company on a clear growth trajectory and built key stakeholder relationships . Group Chief Executive Dr Blessing Mudavanhu will also retire effective December 31, 2023 after five years at the helm, with the board citing his leadership as instrumental in restoring the company's brand and reputation and driving growth strategy including the successful acquisition of a significant shareholding in a leading publicly listed insurance group . Mr Lawrence Nyazema, currently Managing Director of CBZ Bank Limited since January 1, 2022 and previously Executive Director of Wholesale Banking from January 2020, has been appointed acting Group CEO, bringing 19 years of experience at Barclays Bank (now First Capital Bank) and a 10-year prior stint at ZB Financial Holdings . The proposed merger follows CBZ's 2020 acquisition of NSSA's 37.79 percent ZB Financial Holdings shareholding in exchange for CBZ shares worth $640 million (US$7.8 million), comprising 14.341 million new CBZ shares valued at $640,041,800 representing a 2.15 percent stake, and US$11,646,889 cash consideration after transaction costs . Sources indicate the merger scope may extend to include First Mutual Holdings (FMHL), in which CBZ acquired a 31.22 percent stake in September 2023 bringing total shareholding to 36.22 percent, to create Zimbabwe's largest financial services group with five divisions: banking, insurance, investment, property and agriculture, rivaling Old Mutual Zimbabwe . Zembe, appointed ZB Financial Holdings chairman three months prior, holds an MBA from Henley International Business School, is a Chartered Fellow of the CIPD (UK), and brings over 30 years experience including RBZ Advisory Board, Monetary Policy Committees, Standard Chartered Bank executive positions, and senior roles in manufacturing, mining and retail . In his December 2023 interview, Zembe outlined short-term goals of strengthening market share and risk management, medium-term goals of integrating FMHL and ZB to unlock bancassurance value, and long-term goals of regional expansion and sustainable growth, stating CBZ's ownership structure is publicly available and sanctions have had limited impact on operations . The leadership changes were formally ratified at the 32nd Annual General Meeting held July 19, 2024, with shareholders approving Holtzman's retirement, Mudavanhu's retirement, Zembe's reappointment as Independent Non-Executive Director effective December 1, 2023, and Nyazema's appointment as Group CEO effective April 19, 2024

headline: ‘SeedCo Int stocks enough to meet regional demand’
content: Seed Co International announced adequate seed stocks to satisfy regional demand following strong yields in the 2022 production season. The group operates across 14 African countries including Angola, Botswana, DRC, Ethiopia, Ghana, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Nigeria, Rwanda, South Africa, Tanzania, and Zambia. H1 FY2024 (ended Sept 30, 2023) results showed revenue up 23% to USD31.3 million [citation:1][citation:7], with interim operating profit of USD1.3 million rebounding from prior year's USD2.6 million loss [citation:4][citation:9]. Maize sales grew in Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Tanzania, and Zambia driven by firm grain prices. Improved margins and overhead containment contributed to profitability. Net finance costs increased due to higher interest rates and local currency borrowing to manage forex risk. Inventory at peak levels for summer selling season. New climate-smart varieties being released from R&D investment. Mixed rainfall forecast: normal-to-above normal in East Africa, below-normal in Southern Africa (Nov 2023-Jan 2024). Group prepared with diversified footprint and climate-smart portfolio. Total stocks 31,600 tonnes, 36% higher than prior year

headline: "Seeds of Hope: Seed Co Int Narrows HY-Loss"
content: Seed Co International reduced its H1 2024 loss after tax to US$3.9 million (from US$4.3 million in H1 2023). Revenue increased 23% to US$31.3 million driven by improved volume growth and favorable product mix. Operating profit turned positive to US$1.3 million (from a US$2.6 million loss). The company anticipates a return to profitability for FY2023. Operations span multiple African countries (Botswana, Zambia, Malawi, Kenya, Tanzania, South Africa). Challenges include recurring droughts in Sub-Saharan Africa, geopolitical tensions, high fertilizer prices, and governance issues in Malawi causing FX shortages. The company has invested in short-term varieties and drought-tolerant crops (e.g., SC555). The article suggests Seed Co could diversify into mining like Padenga Holdings to reduce agricultural dependency.

headline: New plant increases CAFCA energy consumption 16pc
content: CAFCA Limited's energy intensity rose 16 percent in the year ended September 30, 2023 compared to full year 2022, owing to the installation of an electrolysis plant strategically introduced to enable the organisation to stop outsourcing conversion of copper anodes into starter sheets (raw material requirements mainly imported), although the plant does not directly contribute toward dispatched finished product. Production output dropped 12 percent while energy consumption increased 6 percent. About 92 percent of total energy needed is in the form of electricity, with remaining 8 percent from liquid petroleum gas, diesel and petrol transformed from kWh to gigajoules for computation. Inflation-adjusted acquisitions of property, plants and equipment rose 346.41 percent to $1.16 billion from $260.15 million in FY2022. Revenue surged 118.66 percent to $164 billion from $75 billion, reflecting volatility in exchange rates, sales mix changes and copper price movements. Profit after tax increased 558.20 percent to $51.3 billion from $7.8 billion previously. Most of CAFCA's topline comes from the domestic market. This sustainability disclosure follows CAFCA's strong performance including 43 percent volume increase in 2021 driving turnover up 293 percent (CAFCA007) and the company's strategic positioning as key supplier to ZETDC (CAFCA004)

headline: Delta revenue surges 19pc
content: Afdis volumes decline 12% on grey market activity","Delta Corporation reported a 19 percent surge in revenue in US dollar terms for the quarter ended December 31, 2023 (12 percent year-to-date), driven by robust volume growth across all business units and a strategic focus on US dollar sales which averaged above 70 percent for the year, with lager beer business surpassing historical monthly peak volumes achieving 15 percent quarterly increase and 14 percent for nine months following commissioning of a new packaging line in August 2023 and introduction of returnable glass bottles. Sorghum beer overall volume including exports increased 3 percent for the quarter and 4 percent year-to-date, though domestic sales dipped 5 percent due to increased lager beer availability, with the new Chibuku Super plant in Harare stabilising supply for local and regional markets, while UNB SA recorded 10 percent quarterly volume increase and Natbrew Zambia delivered 23 percent quarterly and 50 percent year-to-date volume surge before slowing post-October price increase amid cholera outbreak, rising maize costs and Kwacha depreciation. Sparkling beverage category maintained strong volume recovery surging 38 percent for the quarter and 25 percent year-to-date, achieving the highest monthly volume ever recorded in December 2023, though facing headwinds from unavoidable price hikes due to impending sugar tax implementation. Associate company African Distillers Limited recorded 12 percent volume decline in the quarter, hit by increased grey market activity for key brands and weaker uptake from formal partners due to account management issues and market pricing distortions, with plant breakdowns and utility disruptions further hampering production. Associate Schweppes recorded 16 percent volume surge riding its new plant commissioned in November 2023 for bottled water and Minute Maid juices, though sugar tax threatens volume recovery through price hikes. Nampak Zimbabwe reported 8 percent overall volume increase lifted by paper product exports and higher demand for plastic packaging from beverage sector. The board has adopted US dollars for presenting 2024 financial statements effective October 2023 to reflect current operating environment and comply with IFRS, noting inherent limitations in translating Zimbabwe dollar financial information and potential discrepancies between CPI, exchange rates and actual transaction rates

headline: CBZH to make mandatory offer to FMHL shareholders
content: First Mutual Holdings Limited notified shareholders that CBZ Holdings Limited is preparing to publish a detailed circular with specific details of its mandatory offer, following the completion of CBZHL's acquisition of 226,997,219 FMHL shares from the National Social Security Authority—representing 31.22 percent of FMHL. CBZHL operates within four main clusters (banking, insurance, investment and agro-insurance) while FMHL has diverse interests in Insurance, Funeral Services and Microfinance. In accordance with the Zimbabwe Stock Exchange Listing Requirements and the Companies and Other Business Entities Act (Chapter 24:31), CBZHL is required to make a mandatory offer to all remaining FMHL shareholders given its shareholding has triggered the 35 percent threshold. Shareholders were advised to exercise caution when dealing in the company's securities pending publication of the offer. However, subsequent developments fundamentally altered this trajectory: The Competition and Tariff Commission made its final decision on November 29, 2024, resolving that CBZHL must maintain only the 31.22 percent shareholding initially approved by the commission . In light of this decision, CBZHL announced on December 9, 2024 that it would no longer be proceeding with the mandatory offer to minority shareholders in FMHL . CBZHL group chief governance officer Rumbidzayi Jakanani confirmed: 'In light of the decision by the CTC, Shareholders of FMHL are hereby advised that CBZHL will no longer be proceeding with the Mandatory Offer to the Minority Shareholders in First Mutual Holdings Limited' . Despite this setback, Group CEO Lawrence Nyazema stated the decision would not frustrate regional market penetration plans, with CBZ targeting to be operating in two regional countries by June 30, 2025 through its subsidiaries including asset management business Datvest

headline: Chitown man hits jackpot in Afdis promotion
content: African Distillers Limited presented a US$1,200 generator to Mr Trymore Sithole of Chitungwiza as the winner of the Viceroy promotion draw, with the lucky consumer describing the win as a life-changing event for himself and his family after purchasing a single box of Viceroy for US$80 during the festive season. AFDIS sales representative Mr Frank Chitiyo confirmed the Viceroy promotion was run nationwide and open to all Zimbabweans with winners chosen at random from eligible entries. Concurrently, the company is running the Gold Blend Land of Gold Promotion offering three fully serviced residential stands in Nyabira, along with 10 refrigerators, 10 full gas stove sets and other prizes, requiring consumers to purchase any Gold Blend Standard or Gold Blend Number 9 whiskey products and send the unique code on the product to enter the draw. This promotion builds on Afdis's established brand activation strategy following the successful 4th Street localisation (AFDIS014-017), the US$1 million cider fermentation plant commissioning (AFDIS010, AFDIS017), and the company's sustained volume growth trajectory which saw 11 percent overall volume growth in H1 2023 (AFDIS018). The Land of Gold Promotion runs from November 20, 2023 to February 29, 2024

headline: Delta Beverages unveil US$3m PSL sponsorship deal
content: Delta Beverages renewed its Premier Soccer League sponsorship for three years (2024-2026) with a total package of US$3.39 million. Allocation: Castle Lager to sponsor the championship race with US$2.265 million (US$755,000 per season); Chibuku Super Cup receives US$1.125 million (US$375,000 per season) [citation:article]. The announcement follows Delta's long-standing football sponsorship, having supported the PSL since 1962 with a brief hiatus, resuming in 2011. Marketing Director Irimayi Muzorewa previously stated: 'Year after year our sponsorship gives us a unique opportunity to present our beer consumers with an activity that they as a nation love.' [citation:DLTA130]. The deal maintains prior funding levels and reinforces Delta's position as football's most committed corporate partner. For Delta, this builds brand visibility (Castle Lager, Chibuku Super) among mass-market consumers, aligns with national passion, and generates community goodwill."

headline: Former deputy Minister Karoro fraud trial opens
content: The fraud trial of former Deputy Minister Douglas Karoro and GMB Mushumbi Pools assistant manager Lovejoy Ngowe opened on February 12, 2024, with GMB regional enterprise risk officer Norman Severa testifying. The pair allegedly misappropriated agricultural inputs worth US$43,000 under the Presidential Inputs Scheme in March 2023. Severa testified that trucks used were not contracted by GMB, violating procedure, and there were no records on beneficiary lists. Karoro and Ngowe face additional charges from 2022 involving US$18,030 worth of maize seed and US$25,000 worth of vegetable combo kits. Karoro was dismissed as Deputy Minister in June 2022 following his arrest. The case highlights systemic corruption risks in agricultural input distribution, with subsequent government responses including blacklisting officials involved in abuse (2024) and introducing a digital credit card system (2025) to bypass centralised depots and enhance transparency. For ZSE-listed agribusinesses, this reinforces reputational risks of government programme participation but also opportunities in formalised, transparent distribution channels.

headline: "Prices Up - Volumes Down, OK Zim Sails in Murky Waters"
content: "OK Zimbabwe reported Q3 2024 volumes down 32% (28% YTD) due to stringent supplier payment terms on ZWL purchases and credit limitations on FX purchases, impacting stock availability. Inflation-adjusted revenue grew 50% (quarter) and 57% YTD to ZWL1.6 trillion. The informal sector creates uneven competition due to regulatory fees not equally enforced. Informal retailers offer cash USD payments to suppliers, while formal retailers offer credit in local currency. The removal of the 10% margin cap on instore exchange rate and IMTT tax on card transactions is expected to help. A survey indicates OK Zimbabwe is becoming more favorable in pricing. Peer Pick n Pay recorded 5% volume growth, suggesting OK's underperformance is due to management and planning issues. OK recognizes the need to adapt to the changing environment."

headline: Delta Beverages renew PSL sponsorship
content: Delta Beverages announced a US$3.39 million three-year sponsorship renewal with the Premier Soccer League (2024-2026), at US$1.13 million per season (maintaining prior level). The deal includes continued sponsorship of the Chibuku Super Cup and Castle Challenge Cup. Marketing Director Irimayi Muzorewa stated: 'Year after year our sponsorship gives us a unique opportunity to present our beer consumers with an activity that they as a nation love.' PSL Chairman Farai Jere described it as continuation of a 'remarkable journey' since 1962 (founding of top-flight league). ZIFA Normalisation Committee Chairman Lincoln Mutasa called Delta 'all-weather friends in football' and 'Mr. Football.' The sponsorship maintains Delta's long-standing football investment (since 1962, with brief hiatus, resumed 2011). For Delta, the deal reinforces brand visibility (Castle Lager, Chibuku Super) among mass-market consumers, aligns with national passion, and builds community goodwill. FY2024 results (March 2024) showed revenue +54% (inflation-adjusted), with sorghum beer contributing 30% of revenue.

headline: ‘Second Republic has confidence in the youth’
content: Former CBZ Agro-Yield MD Dr John Basera appointed Permanent Secretary, led agricultural transformation","Local Government and Public Works Permanent Secretary Dr John Basera, who was invited to join the public service in November 2019 while serving as managing director for CBZ Agro-Yield at age 32, has credited President Mnangagwa's Second Republic for entrusting a 33-year-old in 2020 to lead the transformation of Zimbabwe's agricultural sector, stating he did not think twice about serving the nation having reached the pinnacle of his professional career at a young age at CBZ Holdings. Dr Basera led the team that crafted and implemented the Agriculture and Food Systems Transformation Strategy (2020-2024) which catapulted Zimbabwe's agriculture industry from a US$5.2 billion sector prior to 2020 to US$9.9 billion by September 2023—a feat envisaged for 2026—and lifted Zimbabwe from being a net food importer by end of 2020 to net food exporter by 2022, growing maize production by 200 percent from below 907,000 tonnes in 2019 to a record 2.7 million tonnes by end of 2022 against national annual requirement of 2.2 million tonnes, bumping wheat production from 92,000 tonnes in 2019 to 400,000 tonnes in 2023 against annual requirement of 360,000 tonnes, growing livestock from 5 million to 5.5 million, raw milk production from 75 million litres in 2019 to over 100 million litres by December 2023, reducing milk imports from 9 million kilogrammes in 2019 to less than 4 million kilogrammes, boosting tobacco production and exports from 212 million kilogrammes in 2019 to 297 million kilogrammes in 2023, horticulture export value from US$10 million in 2016 to over US$70 million in 2023, and cotton lint exports from US$15 million prior to 2019 to over US$80 million by end of 2023. The ZimAgric Team under his leadership spearheaded structured performance guarantees which formed the basis for banks including CBZ to on-lend to farmers without pre-requisite collateral, increasing bank participation in agricultural financing and significantly reducing non-performing loans, consummated mechanisation facilities worth over US$200 million with Government importing over 4,400 tractors, and launched the Zimbabwe Mercantile Exchange establishing market-driven price discovery. Dr Basera, born 1986 in Burma Valley, holds a PhD in Agribusiness Development, MBA and BSc Agriculture Honours, is an award-winning leader with Presidential Performance Excellence Awards two years running, ICDZ Lifetime Achievement Award, and has topped the influential 40 under 40 list since 2020, and is an entrepreneur with interests in farming, energy, agribusiness, retailing and transport sectors employing over 250 people, having been one of the top maize growers since 2017/18

headline: Zimswitch accelerates digital payments adoption in Zimbabwe as it celebrates 30-year milestone
content: CBZ confirmed as founding partner","Zimswitch, Zimbabwe's national electronic payments switch designated by the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe in 2020, celebrated its 30th anniversary with a brand refresh, reporting exponential growth in transaction volumes and now processing more than 75 percent of all card-based transactions in the country . The company was established in 1994 through a partnership of six founding financial institutions including Beverly Building Society (now merged into CBZ), Barclays Bank (now First Capital Bank), CABS, Founders Building Society (now ZB Bank), Stanbic and Zimbank (now ZB Bank), with CBZ and Time Bank joining the partnership in 1995 and 1997 respectively, followed by Jewel Bank (also now CBZ) . This historical ownership structure confirms CBZ's foundational role in building Zimbabwe's national payment infrastructure, eliminating unnecessary industry competition through payment infrastructure sharing—a collaboration that solved the permeating challenge of scarce ATMs and Point of Sale infrastructure, with the first ATM and POS transactions processed in 1995 and 1996 respectively . The milestone coverage also detailed Zimswitch's recent innovations including Contactless Card NFC technology (2021), ZEEPAY bulk payment EFT solution (2021), 3D secure e-commerce platform (2023), multi-currency and ZiG processing, and a solar power station commissioned in 2019 to power electronic transactions through green energy with excess fed into the national grid . While this article primarily celebrates Zimswitch's achievements, CBZ's explicit identification as both an original founder and subsequent acquirer of merging institutions represents positive brand association with Zimbabwe's most critical financial infrastructure, reinforcing the bank's systemic importance to the national payments ecosystem.

headline: Zembe takes over as substantive CBZ chair
content: CBZ Holdings has appointed Mr Luxon Zembe as substantive chairman effective April 19, 2024, having served in an acting capacity since December 1, 2023 following the retirement of Mr Marc Holtzman at the beginning of December 2023. Zembe, a renowned banker, strategist and businessman with over 30 years' experience consulting for reputable private, public and non-governmental organisations locally and regionally, has been appointed to superintend the proposed merger of CBZ with ZB Financial Holdings, pursuant to the strategic vision of creating one of the country's largest financial services groups. His corporate experience includes executive positions in manufacturing, mining, financial and commercial sectors, service on the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe advisory board and monetary policy committees, and executive positions at Standard Chartered Bank. CBZ stated that his corporate leadership, moral compass and integrity have stood the test of time, positioning him to provide strategic leadership. Zembe was chairman of ZB Financial Holdings from August 1, 2023 to April 14, 2024, having joined the ZBFH board as independent non-executive director on September 21, 2022. The proposed merger follows CBZ's 2020 acquisition of ZB Financial Holdings, in which NSSA disposed of its 37.79 percent ZBFH shareholding in exchange for CBZ shares worth $640 million (US$7.8 million), receiving 14.341 million new CBZ shares valued at $640,041,800 (2.15 percent stake) as 50 percent consideration and US$11,646,889 cash for the balance after transaction costs. Zembe holds an MBA from Henley International Business School, is a Chartered Fellow of the CIPD (UK), Fellow of the Institute of Directors, Fellow of the Zimbabwe Institute of Management, past president of the African Chamber of Commerce and founding vice president of the Pan African Chamber of Commerce and Industry. This appointment formalises the leadership transition first announced in December 2023 (CBZ079) and follows Holtzman's four-year tenure during which CBZ's market capitalisation re-rated from US$14 million to over US$500 million and the bank was cleared of a US$385 million OFAC penalty (CBZ098, CBZ107).

headline: Over 1 200 students benefit from Delta bursary programme
content: Delta Corporation's annual bursary programme has supported over 1,200 students over 20+ years, with approximately US$2 million invested to date . The programme targets vulnerable but academically strong students at A-Level and university levels, selected with assistance from the Ministry of Primary and Secondary Education . In 2024, 200 students are beneficiaries . The initiative is complemented by the Delta Technical Institute (DTI), which has graduated over 1,500 artisans. GM Corporate Affairs Patricia Murambinda stated: 'Delta Corporation aspires for a better world by contributing to making a difference in the communities that we operate in. We believe in a brighter, better, thriving and growing society.' The programme builds long-term brand equity, demonstrates corporate citizenship, and creates a talent pipeline for the group and the broader economy. For investors, this represents strategic investment in social license to operate and sustainable community relationships.

headline: Castle Tankard on cards
content: The 2024 Castle Tankard, Africa's oldest sponsored horse racing event, is scheduled for May 11 at Borrowdale Park and Racecourse with a 12-horse field. Defending champion Ideal View (Swanson yard) headlines, with Wantage and Citrudal (Stidolph yard) among contenders [citation:article]. Total prize money: US$50,000, with winner receiving US$25,000 [citation:article]. Delta Beverages, through its Castle Lager brand, has sponsored the event for decades. The race is part of Delta's extensive sports marketing portfolio, which includes the Premier Soccer League, Castle Challenge Cup, Chibuku Super Cup, and Chibuku Neshamwari [citation:DLTA130, DLTA097]. The event builds brand visibility, reinforces Castle Lager's association with prestige and heritage, and engages the horse racing community. For Delta, this represents sustained investment in brand equity and community engagement.

headline: "CAFCA Appoints New CEO, Starafrica Adds Executive Board Member"
content: CAFCA appointed Vimbai Nyakudya, a qualified Chartered Accountant, as Chief Executive Officer effective June 3, 2024. Nyakudya's leadership is expected to bring a happy and successful future for the company. CAFCA manufactures over 900 cabling products to British, South African, and Zimbabwean standards. It recovers decommissioned cables for recycling and supplies telecommunication cable. Established in 1947, CAFCA is a subsidiary of CBi Electric African Cables (South Africa), owned by Reunert Limited (South Africa). It was listed on the ZSE on May 1, 1997. Separately, Starafrica Corporation appointed Odiline Kava as a Non-Executive Director effective June 1, 2024. Kava brings over 30 years of experience in strategic leadership and business development, with a track record in brand-building.

headline: Delta review pool champs purse
content: Delta Beverages announced increased prize money for the 2024 Carling Black Label national pool tournament, with winners receiving US$2,500 (up from US$1,500 in 2023). Runners-up: US$1,500; third: US$1,000; fourth: US$500 [citation:article]. The tournament, run in conjunction with the Zimbabwe Pool Association (ZIPA) for over 10 years, attracts at least 2,000 players from across Zimbabwe. Provincial qualifying rounds began June 2024, with finals scheduled for July 27 in Harare [citation:article]. 2023 winners Lindani Gumpo (men) and Patricia Ganya (women) each received US$1,500. Delta stated the tournament is 'gaining traction' [citation:article]. The sponsorship builds brand visibility for Carling Black Label, engages grassroots communities, and demonstrates Delta's long-term commitment to sport development. For Delta, this is part of a diversified sports marketing portfolio (football, rugby, horse racing, traditional dance) that builds brand equity across consumer segments."

headline: Delta sees volume growth after US$71m capex in 2023
content: Delta sees volume growth after US$71m capex in 2023; Afdis receives US$1m, revenue hits US$768m","Delta Corporation expects significant volume growth in the current financial year following the group's US$71 million investment in 2023 to boost production capacity, allocated as US$15 million into lager beer operations, US$20 million into Chibuku Super, US$10 million into a soft drinks PET line, US$5 million into the vehicle fleet, US$15 million into containers, and US$1 million into its associate company, Afdis . This capex programme has bolstered Delta's daily production capacity to approximately 350,000 litres and raised national beer packaging capacity from 2.2 million hectolitres to 2.9 million hectolitres per annum, with the business poised to benefit from enhanced product supply and improved operational efficiencies across segments . For the 2024 financial year, Delta reported revenue of US$768 million (43% growth) and operating income of US$152 million (53% increase), with IH Securities forecasting FY25 revenue of US$824.5 million representing 7.4% year-on-year growth driven by volumes rather than price increases . The group faces headwinds including lower global mineral prices (mining revenue expected to fall 10% in 2024 following a 7.6% decline in 2023), El Niño-induced drought reducing agricultural output, and power shortages, though these negatives are expected to be offset by increased mining output, resilient diaspora remittances, and gold's record-high prices . Delta's farmer-centric approach continues with over 11,500 contracted farmers targeting 100% skilled, connected and financially empowered status by 2025 . IH Securities notes Delta trades at a P/E multiple of 6.7x versus peer average of 17.3x, though the group faces US$46 million in sugar tax liability on top of US$142 million in traditional taxes for FY24 . The broader economic context saw Zimbabwe's agriculture sector contract 18.12% in 2024 with cotton production plummeting to a record low of 13,000 tonnes from 82,000 tonnes, while mining showed 12.9% growth .

headline: Delta sees volume growth after US$71m capex in 2023
content: Delta sees volume growth after US$71m capex in 2023; Afdis receives US$1m, revenue hits US$768m","Delta Corporation expects significant volume growth in the current financial year following the group's US$71 million investment in 2023 to boost production capacity, allocated as US$15 million into lager beer operations, US$20 million into Chibuku Super, US$10 million into a soft drinks PET line, US$5 million into the vehicle fleet, US$15 million into containers, and US$1 million into its associate company, Afdis . This capex programme has bolstered Delta's daily production capacity to approximately 350,000 litres and raised national beer packaging capacity from 2.2 million hectolitres to 2.9 million hectolitres per annum, with the business poised to benefit from enhanced product supply and improved operational efficiencies across segments . For the 2024 financial year, Delta reported revenue of US$768 million (43% growth) and operating income of US$152 million (53% increase), with IH Securities forecasting FY25 revenue of US$824.5 million representing 7.4% year-on-year growth driven by volumes rather than price increases . The group faces headwinds including lower global mineral prices (mining revenue expected to fall 10% in 2024 following a 7.6% decline in 2023), El Niño-induced drought reducing agricultural output, and power shortages, though these negatives are expected to be offset by increased mining output, resilient diaspora remittances, and gold's record-high prices . Delta's farmer-centric approach continues with over 11,500 contracted farmers targeting 100% skilled, connected and financially empowered status by 2025 . IH Securities notes Delta trades at a P/E multiple of 6.7x versus peer average of 17.3x, though the group faces US$46 million in sugar tax liability on top of US$142 million in traditional taxes for FY24 . The broader economic context saw Zimbabwe's agriculture sector contract 18.12% in 2024 with cotton production plummeting to a record low of 13,000 tonnes from 82,000 tonnes, while mining showed 12.9% growth .

headline: OK Zimbabwe comes to the rescue of yesteryear TV presenter John Phiri
content: OK Zimbabwe presented a US$500 shopping voucher to renowned yesteryear TV presenter John 'Mzala' Phiri through its community initiative, the Trolley for Trolley Challenge, held in Glen View [citation:article]. Phiri, a legendary figure in Zimbabwean broadcasting (ZBC's 'Ezomgido' and 'Mutinhimira weMumhanzi'), recently suffered a stroke affecting his speech and is a loyal customer at the OK Makomva branch [citation:article]. The initiative brought joy to the Glen View community and strengthened neighbourhood bonds. OK Zimbabwe noted Phiri's historical role in the company's marketing, having conducted live broadcasts from OK stores nationwide and anchored the OK Grand Challenge Race Days for years [citation:article]. The gesture reaffirms OK Zimbabwe's commitment to community and appreciation for long-standing relationships. For investors, this demonstrates authentic community engagement and brand values in action.

headline: "Seed Co Leverages Poor Sales with Exports, Introduces New Resistant Varieties"
content: "Seed Co reported FY2024 (ended March 31, 2024) local maize seed sales nearly a third lower due to El Niño drought. The company leveraged increased exports to offset poor domestic sales and reduce inventories. New drought-resistant varieties launched: SC661 (high-yielding, disease tolerance, stay-green) and SC657 (yield potential up to 16 tonnes/ha, 138-145 days maturity). Wheat sales volumes remained constant; new high-yielding wheat variety SC W9104 introduced. Revenue declined 10% to ZWL813.66 billion, but cost of sales decreased and other income increased to ZWL1.2 trillion due to exchange gains on USD receivables. Operating profit reached ZWL1.1 trillion. PAT increased 8% to ZWL463.34 billion, driven by exchange gains and cost management. The 2024/2025 La Niña season is expected to bring more rainfall. Future strategy focuses on export expansion and USD-denominated sales."

headline: Presidents Mnangagwa, Nyusi tour stands at agric show
content: President Mnangagwa and Mozambican President Filipe Nyusi toured exhibition stands at the Zimbabwe Agricultural Show on August 27, 2024, ahead of the official opening presided over by President Nyusi. The tour included the Chinhoyi University of Technology, SeedCo, mining village, livestock village, and ARDA exhibition stands. The visit by two heads of state underscores the strategic importance of agriculture and agribusiness in Zimbabwe-Mozambique bilateral relations. For SeedCo, direct presidential attention validates its position as a regional leader in seed technology and food security. The agricultural show provides a platform for SeedCo to showcase new climate-resilient varieties (SC673 maize, SC SZ08 soybean, SC W9104 wheat) to regional policymakers and potential buyers from Mozambique. The visit aligns with SeedCo's export strategy targeting regional seed deficits and its cautious optimism about future growth. For the agribusiness sector, high-level political attention signals continued government support for commercial agriculture and food security initiatives.

headline: Take Chibuku Super Cup seriously: Jere
content: "The 2024 Chibuku Super Cup tournament begins August 7 with preliminary round matches at Mandava, Zvishavane. Delta Beverages sponsors the tournament with US$375,000 [citation:article]. PSL President Farai Jere encouraged clubs to take the competition seriously as the winner qualifies for the CAF Confederation Cup, providing continental exposure and a pathway to African club football [citation:article]. Jere noted that decentralizing matches across venues (including Wadzanai, Rufaro, Sakubva, The Heart Stadium) has increased attendance by over 50%, enhancing brand visibility for sponsors [citation:article]. Dynamos are defending champions and will face Zesco in Confederation Cup qualification [citation:article]. Jere expressed gratitude: 'We are grateful to Delta Beverages for being there during all these difficult years.' [citation:article] The sponsorship is part of Delta's broader football investment, which includes the Premier Soccer League (US$2.265m) and Castle Challenge Cup [citation:DLTA130]

headline: ZiG swipe deals cost OK Zimbabwe ZiG 500k
content: "Armshell Tagwida (27), OK Zimbabwe's Chegutu branch manager, appeared in court charged with fraudulently crediting ZiG 574,318.45 onto four corporate Shop Easy debit cards between January and August 2024 without proof of deposit, using another employee's password [citation:article]. The cards were given to Bindura till supervisor Takundanashe Nhemwa (fugitive), who swiped groceries worth ZiG 525,173.25 at OK Bindura [citation:article]. Crime discovered during financial statement checks; CCTV identified Nhemwa. US$782 was recovered from Tagwida's house, and ZiG 44,009.28 in uncollected groceries were recovered [citation:article]. The amount represents approximately 0.04% of OK Zimbabwe's annualized revenue (based on Q1 2024 revenue of ZiG 2.9bn). Swift detection and recovery demonstrate internal controls functioning. For investors, this is an isolated incident with immaterial financial impact.

headline: "CBZ Holdings' Half-Year Profit Nearly Halves"
content: "CBZ Holdings reported H1 2024 PAT of ZWG 656.3 million, a 45.5% decline from ZWG 1.2 billion in H1 2023. Total income declined 41.4% to ZWG 1.7 billion (from ZWG 2.9 billion). Non-interest income plummeted 57.7% to ZWG 1.1 billion (from ZWG 2.6 billion). Deposits declined 6.5% to ZWG 11.5 billion. Loans and advances increased 2.2% to ZWG 4.7 billion. The group maintained a robust capital base of ZWG 17.59 billion. The currency transition from ZWL to ZiG on April 5, 2024, had a profound impact on financial performance, leading to accounting adjustments. Chairman Luxon Zembe stated the group will continue to reconfigure its business model to unlock long-term value. Anticipated improvements in the agricultural sector and continued public infrastructure investments are expected to drive future growth."

headline: 2024 Chibuku Road To Fame search in full throttle
content: "Delta Beverages' 2024 Chibuku Road to Fame talent search is underway, running September 28 - November 9 under the theme 'Bviravira, Tshay'iNgoma', in partnership with the National Arts Council of Zimbabwe [citation:article]. Provincial finals completed: Harare (The Outlaws), Matabeleland South (Fugues Band), Midlands (The Cousin), Mashonaland Central (Shanty Town). Provincial prizes: US$2,500 (winner), US$1,500 (second), US$1,000 (third) [citation:article]. National finale: November 9 in Harare with prizes US$15,000 + recording contract (winner), US$7,500 (second), US$5,000 (third) [citation:article]. Previous winners include 911 Band (2023), Romeo Gasa, Club Shanga, Munandi Express, Terry & Bliss [citation:article]. The programme, running for over two decades, demonstrates Delta's sustained commitment to arts development, youth engagement, and cultural preservation. For Delta, this builds deep brand equity, authentic community connections, and alignment with national arts policy.

headline: Blue Chip counters drive ZSE gains on Monday
content: On October 21, 2024, the ZSE All Share Index rose 1.58% (+4.20 points) to 269.39, driven by gains in select blue-chip counters. CAFCA led with ZiG 160.0000c gain to 1560.0000c. Econot gained ZiG 52.6880c to 496.6125c. TSL added ZiG 30.0000c to 280.0000c. First Mutual gained ZiG 25.0000c to 430.0000c. SeedCo declined ZiG 76.5809c to 458.1000c. RioZim reported challenging HY results: revenue -19% to US$282.53m, gross profit -72% to US$3.89m, operating loss -28% to US$159.42m, loss before tax US$169.17m, no dividend. Tigere REIT reported strong Q1: net property income +2% to US$1.31m, comprehensive income +6% to US$854,413, 100% occupancy, average rent +8% to US$19.24, declared US$0.03476 dividend per share. Market momentum reflected continued confidence in blue chips, though mixed performance highlighted broader economic challenges.

headline: BAT Reports Dismal HY Performance, Pays Over 100 Million in Taxes"
content: "BAT Zimbabwe reported dismal H1 2024 performance with revenue down 38% to ZWG248 million, driven by 9% decline in sales volumes. Despite posting a net loss of ZWG175.95 million, the company paid over ZWG100 million in taxes (excise duty, corporate tax, VAT, customs duties, PAYE, withholding taxes). Gross profit decreased 41% to ZWG200.89 million. Operating loss widened to ZWG145 million (from ZWG61 million). Net loss ballooned to ZWG175.95 million (from ZWG38.41 million). Cost optimization efforts yielded results: production costs reduced to ZWG47 million (from ZWG60 million), administrative expenses to ZWG33 million (from ZWG42 million). The operating landscape was characterized by monetary instability and substantial exchange rate distortions. Challenges include excessive taxes, high operating costs, and rampant smuggling of cheap cigarettes. Nearly half of revenue was eaten up by taxes."

headline: "BAT's New Strategic Focus: Cost Optimization to Mitigate Earnings Crunch"
content: BAT Zimbabwe sustained cost reduction initiatives in the 9 months to September 2024, reducing operating costs by 33%. In H1 2024, production costs reduced to ZWG47 million (from ZWG60 million) and administrative expenses to ZWG33 million (from ZWG42 million). Sales volumes declined 8% y/y due to inflationary pressures and currency volatility. Domestic cigarette revenue plummeted 37% y/y due to decreased consumer spending. The company faces challenges from influx of new brands, dumping of low-cost alternatives, high taxes, electricity tariffs, export surrender requirements, and power outages. Strategic responses include dual currency billing for customer flexibility and revamped Route-to-Consumer operations to create a responsive, future-fit model. The cost management strategy is crucial for navigating one of its most challenging phases.

headline: "Pathway to Recovery: BAT's Q3 Under Glance"
content: "BAT Zimbabwe reported Q3 2024 sales volumes declined 9%, revenue fell 38% to ZWG248 million. The company last registered after-tax profitability in 2021 and has since encountered continuous losses. Performance impacted by influx of low-cost cigarette brands from neighboring countries, new market entrants, high taxes (ZIMRA), frequent power outages, and rising electricity tariffs. ZESA increased tariffs three times in the past year. In H1 2024, BAT paid ZWG112 million in taxes (~50% of revenue). Cost reduction strategies are being implemented: operating costs reduced 33% in Q3. Production costs reduced to ZWG47 million (from ZWG60 million), administrative expenses to ZWG33 million (from ZWG42 million). To counter low-cost competition, BAT must enhance promotional activities and capitalize on pricing strategies to increase brand visibility.

headline: Double Trouble for Delta's Beer: Sorghum Struggles at Home, Abroad
content: "Delta Corporation's H1 2025 sorghum beer sales declined: Zimbabwe -11% (including ceased exports, domestic -3%), South Africa -8% (due to industrial action), Zambia -20% (due to severe load shedding). Despite commissioning a new Chibuku Super Plant in SA, volumes declined. Delta's market share in opaque beer slipped to 87% (from 92%) following Innscor's Nyathi launch. Sorghum beer category is vulnerable to drought-induced reduced disposable incomes and cost pressures on key cereals. Competition intensifying from alternative alcohol categories and smuggling activities. Delta maintains competitive advantage due to scale (monthly capacity 400,000 hectoliters). Strategic initiatives needed: product diversification, targeted marketing, R&D to address generational changes in consumer preferences. The company's ability to adapt to changing market conditions will determine its success."

headline: CBZ targets unbanked with new financial inclusion initiatives
content: CEO Nyazema outlines informal sector strategy","CBZ Holdings Group Chief Executive Officer Lawrence Nyazema has outlined a strategic focus on financial inclusion targeting Zimbabwe's largely unbanked informal sector—which now constitutes the bulk of economic activity—through developing products and solutions not only through the bank but across all group business units including insurance and agriculture, stating that the banking sector needs to find ways of keeping informal sector deposits to increase overall deposit base, as 'when somebody goes to a fast-food outlet, they take their informal money, they buy fast food, the deposit comes, but the deposit quickly disappears.' This strategy follows CBZ's major strategic shake-up in December 2023 (CBZ079) where Nyazema was appointed acting Group CEO effective December 31, 2023, later formally ratified at the 32nd Annual General Meeting on July 19, 2024, succeeding Dr Blessing Mudavanhu who retired after five years. The initiative aligns with the second phase of the National Financial Inclusion Strategy (NFIS 2022-2026) and leverages financial technology to serve underserved and marginalised groups including women, youths, MSMEs, rural communities, smallholder farmers, people with disabilities, pensioners and the elderly. Experts cited note Zimbabwe's high cost of compliance incentivises informalisation, with CZI president Mucha Mkanganwi advocating for conditional incentives such as social insurance schemes to make formalisation attractive rather than punitive tax measures, while SME Association CEO Farai Mutambanengwe stated the current tax regime discourages formalisation. This strategic pivot complements CBZ's historical financial inclusion initiatives including the Youth Entrepreneurship Programme (YEP) co-sponsored with CBZ Bank which trained nearly 3,000 youths over three years (CBZ053), the CBZ Schools Debate Championship incorporating primary and secondary schools with junior parliamentary qualification (CBZ014, CBZ023, CBZ042), and the CBZ Marathon promoting healthy lifestyles and community engagement (CBZ027, CBZ034, CBZ038, CBZ047).

headline: "CBZ-FML Acquisition Crumble: Competition and Tariff Commission Ruling Halts Deal"
content: The Competition and Tariff Commission (CTC) ruled against CBZ Holdings' proposed acquisition of additional shares in First Mutual Holdings (FML). CBZ had purchased a 31.2% stake in FML from NSSA in 2023, increasing its total stake to over 36%, which triggered a mandatory offer requirement under ZSE rules. The CTC resolved that CBZHL must maintain only the 31.22% shareholding initially approved. NSSA, the largest shareholder in CBZ with a 23.32% stake, sold part of its 66% stake in FML to CBZ in exchange for cash and 9% of CBZ stock to comply with regulatory limits of ZSE and IPEC. The CTC's role was to assess whether the transaction could significantly reduce competition or create a monopoly. CBZ's strategy to merge with FMHL was part of a broader vision to establish a pan-African bank, enhance shareholder value, and expand regional presence. The ruling nullifies the mandatory offer requirement.
